S.PHON: o~unija to~usa ɾaɾakaw wa miɾəŋo~u to~u o~u maanan ko~u so~uʡɾinaj a pasaɾat, sasamaan miɾifo~uk to~u so~uʡɾinaj pasaɾat, samaan misaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmak to~u ʦito~uɾasaj a pasaɾat, paira to~u əpo~uʦ no~u nijaħ ato~u tao~u.
S.TRANSL: These two articles will consider what true freedom is, how we can attain it, and how to use our relative freedom now to benefit ourselves and others.

S.FORM: Misamaan kiso mipadama to adadaay ato mato'asay tamdaw?
Misamaan kiso mipadama to adadaay ato mato'asay tamdaw?
S.PHON: misamaan kiso~u mipaɬ~ɮama to~u aɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮaaj ato~u mato~uʡasaj tamɬ~ɮaw?
S.TRANSL: How can you assist the sick and the elderly?

S.FORM: Miharateng ko tao takowanan, o misa'icelay micowat to rihaday ato kalacecay han?"
Miharateng ko tao takowanan, o misa'icelay micowat to rihaday ato kalacecay han?"
S.PHON: miħaratəŋ ko~u tao~u tako~uwanan, o~u misaʡiʦəɾaj miʦo~uwat to~u riħaɬ~ɮaj ato~u kaɾaʦəʦaj ħan?"
S.TRANSL: Am I known for going out of my way to promote peace and unity?'

S.FORM: Mararaway a harateng ato waywayan.
Mararaway a harateng ato waywayan.
S.PHON: mararawaj a ħaratəŋ ato~u wajwajan.
S.TRANSL: Ungodly thinking and conduct.

S.FORM: Mapakalalokay ko 'alomanay a salikaka, 'ifowa a mirienang to patirengay ato mitomadaway to kasa'opoan a demak.
Mapakalalokay ko 'alomanay a salikaka, 'ifowa a mirienang to patirengay ato mitomadaway to kasa'opoan a demak.
S.PHON: mapakaɾaɾo~ukaj ko~u ʡaɾo~umanaj a saɾikaka, ʡifo~uwa a miriənaŋ to~u patirəŋaj ato~u mito~umaɬ~ɮawaj to~u kasaʡo~upo~uan a ɬ~ɮəmak.
S.TRANSL: It also encouraged the brothers to become more involved in constructing and maintaining their own Kingdom Halls.

S.FORM: ato timoloay.
ato timoloay.
S.PHON: ato~u timo~uɾo~uaj.
S.TRANSL: when troubled times we've met.

S.FORM: Padamaen cangra tanektek i kimad.
Padamaen cangra tanektek i kimad.
S.PHON: paɬ~ɮamaən ʦaŋra tanəktək i kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: May ev'ry one of them stand firm.

S.FORM: O romaroma wama wina cowaho ka halafin micada to ino', alatek misaaliay to malohemay Krisciyang mipadang a pasifana' to wawa nangra, anca pacekiwen cangra.
O romaroma wama wina cowaho ka halafin micada to ino', alatek misaaliay to malohemay Krisciyang mipadang a pasifana' to wawa nangra, anca pacekiwen cangra.
S.PHON: o~u ro~umaro~uma wama wina ʦo~uwaħo~u ka ħaɾafin miʦaɬ~ɮa to~u ino~uʡ, aɾatək misaaɾiaj to~u maɾo~uħəmaj krisʦijaŋ mipaɬ~ɮaŋ a pasifanaʡ to~u wawa naŋra, anʦa paʦəkiwən ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: On occasion, new ones ask mature publishers to help them teach their children the truth, even to conduct a Bible study with the youngsters.

S.FORM: MAKALIMAY CANGRA MIKINAFALAH, itiniay i Tureky
MAKALIMAY CANGRA MIKINAFALAH, itiniay i Tureky
S.PHON: makaɾimaj ʦan*ra mikinafaɾaħ, itiniaj i to~u~urəkj
S.TRANSL: They Offered Themselves Willingly, In Turkey

S.FORM: Kawra paso'linay cingra to tatimolo cangra, so'lin tahalifa to saco'ay no paro no loma', o mamadimadi cangra.
Kawra paso'linay cingra to tatimolo cangra, so'lin tahalifa to saco'ay no paro no loma', o mamadimadi cangra.
S.PHON: kawra paso~uʡɾinaj ʦiŋra to~u tatimo~uɾo~u ʦaŋra, so~uʡɾin taħaɾifa to~u saʦo~uʡaj no~u paro~u no~u ɾo~umaʡ, o~u mamaɬ~ɮimaɬ~ɮi ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: Yet, he was confident that his disciples would have the courage to maintain their loyalty to him despite family opposition.

S.FORM: Hano mihaen cangra hani?
Hano mihaen cangra hani?
S.PHON: ħano~u miħaən ʦaŋra ħani?
S.TRANSL: What might induce them to do such a thing?

S.FORM: Wa ta'angay ko piako'id no miliyaway ma'orip a tamdaw, papaihkal cangra to sini'adaay i tao.
Wa ta'angay ko piako'id no miliyaway ma'orip a tamdaw, papaihkal cangra to sini'adaay i tao.
S.PHON: wa taʡaŋaj ko~u piako~uʡiɬ~ɮ no~u miɾijawaj maʡo~urip a tamɬ~ɮaw, papaiħkaɾ ʦaŋra to~u siniʡaɬ~ɮaaj i tao~u.
S.TRANSL: Anyone then living who refuses to show kindness and help others will have no permanent place under God's Kingdom.

S.FORM: Miekoan no mirikecay cangra, "miowingay to tamdaw misafelit to sarikec, mana'ayay mala sofitay, misafelit to kitakit, padetengay misafelit to sarikec", tada o ta'akayay a raraw, sanay ko mirikecay.
Miekoan no mirikecay cangra, "miowingay to tamdaw misafelit to sarikec, mana'ayay mala sofitay, misafelit to kitakit, padetengay misafelit to sarikec", tada o ta'akayay a raraw, sanay ko mirikecay.
S.PHON: miəko~uan no~u mirikəʦaj ʦaŋra, "mio~uwiŋaj to~u tamɬ~ɮaw misafəɾit to~u sarikəʦ, manaʡajaj maɾa so~ufitaj, misafəɾit to~u kitakit, paɬ~ɮətəŋaj misafəɾit to~u sarikəʦ", taɬ~ɮa o~u taʡakajaj a raraw, sanaj ko~u mirikəʦaj.
S.TRANSL: They were accused of "unlawfully, feloniously and wilfully causing insubordination, disloyalty and refusal of duty in the military and naval forces of the United States."

S.FORM: Pinaay miheca cangra, misahalaka to mamalinah tayra i misaaliay to mitosilay a pala, kawra cowa ka masa'osi ko harateng ni Perin.
Pinaay miheca cangra, misahalaka to mamalinah tayra i misaaliay to mitosilay a pala, kawra cowa ka masa'osi ko harateng ni Perin.
S.PHON: pinaaj miħəʦa ʦaŋra, misaħaɾaka to~u mamaɾinaħ tajra i misaaɾiaj to~u mito~usiɾaj a paɾa, kawra ʦo~uwa ka masaʡo~usi ko~u ħaratəŋ ni pərin.
S.TRANSL: For years, they had been thinking about expanding their ministry by moving abroad, but Perrine hesitated to move.

S.FORM: Tanamen a misalalicay cangra tono miso a misahalakaan mamaan?
Tanamen a misalalicay cangra tono miso a misahalakaan mamaan?
S.PHON: tanamən a misaɾaɾiʦaj ʦaŋra to~uno~u miso~u a misaħaɾakaan mamaan?
S.TRANSL: Why not discuss your plans with some of them?

S.FORM: O so'linay Krisciyang wa midemak to maanan, ho mahapinang no tao to cowa ka lecad cangra to mihakelongay to mararaway pidoyongan hani?
O so'linay Krisciyang wa midemak to maanan, ho mahapinang no tao to cowa ka lecad cangra to mihakelongay to mararaway pidoyongan hani?
S.PHON: o~u so~uʡɾinaj krisʦijaŋ wa miɬ~ɮəmak to~u maanan, ħo~u maħapinaŋ no~u tao~u to~u ʦo~uwa ka ɾəʦaɬ~ɮ ʦaŋra to~u miħakəɾo~uŋaj to~u mararawaj piɬ~ɮo~ujo~uŋan ħani?
S.TRANSL: What is one way that true Christians distinguish themselves from members of false religions?

S.FORM: Misamacayay cangra mietan to payci anca mitaling to hafay nangra.
Misamacayay cangra mietan to payci anca mitaling to hafay nangra.
S.PHON: misamaʦajaj ʦaŋra miətan to~u pajʦi anʦa mitaɾiŋ to~u ħafaj naŋra.
S.TRANSL: They are focused on accumulating wealth or on holding on to what they have.

S.FORM: Misamaanay hano lipahak cangra hani?
Misamaanay hano lipahak cangra hani?
S.PHON: misamaanaj ħano~u ɾipaħak ʦaŋra ħani?
S.TRANSL: Why are they so happy?

S.FORM: Mihayay cangra takowanan patikol i Miyanmar, kawra cowa ka tangsol kako mihaen, kinapinapina a mitolon nga'misa'osi a mihaen to demak."
Mihayay cangra takowanan patikol i Miyanmar, kawra cowa ka tangsol kako mihaen, kinapinapina a mitolon nga'misa'osi a mihaen to demak."
S.PHON: miħajaj ʦaŋra tako~uwanan patiko~uɾ i mijanmar, kawra ʦo~uwa ka taŋso~uɾ kako~u miħaən, kinapinapina a mito~uɾo~un ŋaʡmisaʡo~usi a miħaən to~u ɬ~ɮəmak."
S.TRANSL: We all felt that I should return to Myanmar, but it took some time and many prayers before I reached my final decision."

S.FORM: Ikor, mala cafayayto cangra milakowit to riyar a mitosil.
Ikor, mala cafayayto cangra milakowit to riyar a mitosil.
S.PHON: iko~ur, maɾa ʦafajajto~u ʦaŋra miɾako~uwit to~u rijar a mito~usiɾ.
S.TRANSL: Soon, those two men began serving together as missionaries.

S.FORM: Micudiyap cangra ikor, patikol tayra i Matakaska a mitosil cangra.
Micudiyap cangra ikor, patikol tayra i Matakaska a mitosil cangra.
S.PHON: miʦo~u~uɬ~ɮijap ʦaŋra iko~ur, patiko~uɾ tajra i matakaska a mito~usiɾ ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: Upon graduating, the couple were delighted to be reassigned to Madagascar.

S.FORM: O naitiniay i roma kitakit tayra mirienang a tamdaw, lipahakay cangra mitosil i Tureky.
O naitiniay i roma kitakit tayra mirienang a tamdaw, lipahakay cangra mitosil i Tureky.
S.PHON: o~u naitiniaj i ro~uma kitakit tajra miriənaŋ a tamɬ~ɮaw, ɾipaħakaj ʦaŋra mito~usiɾ i to~u~urəkj.
S.TRANSL: The foreign participants thoroughly enjoyed the preaching work.

S.FORM: Mihaenay kala 'adaan no mikowanay no Suwiay cangra, mihakokay cangranan.
Mihaenay kala 'adaan no mikowanay no Suwiay cangra, mihakokay cangranan.
S.PHON: miħaənaj kaɾa ʡaɬ~ɮaan no~u miko~uwanaj no~u so~u~uwiaj ʦaŋra, miħako~ukaj ʦaŋranan.
S.TRANSL: Hence, they were persecuted as enemies of the Communist state.

S.FORM: Miki'okay kora 'a'ideman to mamidipot tamiyanan, hato caayto ka pikalisanga cangra tamiyanan.
Miki'okay kora 'a'ideman to mamidipot tamiyanan, hato caayto ka pikalisanga cangra tamiyanan.
S.PHON: mikiʡo~ukaj ko~ura ʡaʡiɬ~ɮəman to~u mamiɬ~ɮipo~ut tamijanan, ħato~u ʦaajto~u ka pikaɾisaŋa ʦaŋra tamijanan.
S.TRANSL: He made sure that police officers stood near our home for a week, and the persecutors gave us no more trouble.

S.FORM: Makalimay Cangra Mikinafalah, Itiniay i Miyanmar
Makalimay Cangra Mikinafalah, Itiniay i Miyanmar
S.PHON: makaɾimaj ʦaŋra mikinafaɾaħ, itiniaj i mijanmar
S.TRANSL: They Offered Themselves Willingly, In Myanmar

S.FORM: So'lin malotay ko roma tamdaw, kamayan mirienang to 'ayaw no mitosilay a sa'opo, mahererek cangra mitosil, patikolay fanekfek ko harateng, na iraay ko mahaenay malifetay iso han?
So'lin malotay ko roma tamdaw, kamayan mirienang to 'ayaw no mitosilay a sa'opo, mahererek cangra mitosil, patikolay fanekfek ko harateng, na iraay ko mahaenay malifetay iso han?
S.PHON: so~uʡɾin maɾo~utaj ko~u ro~uma tamɬ~ɮaw, kamajan miriənaŋ to~u ʡajaw no~u mito~usiɾaj a saʡo~upo~u, maħərərək ʦaŋra mito~usiɾ, patiko~uɾaj fanəkfək ko~u ħaratəŋ, na iraaj ko~u maħaənaj maɾifətaj iso~u ħan?
S.TRANSL: How many times have you gone to a meeting for field service not feeling your best, only to finish preaching that day refreshed and invigorated?

S.FORM: Pakalalokay cangra takowanan ato sasafaay a wawa to fafahiyan nangra ci Nora, mirienang to misakafahay a demak.
Pakalalokay cangra takowanan ato sasafaay a wawa to fafahiyan nangra ci Nora, mirienang to misakafahay a demak.
S.PHON: pakaɾaɾo~ukaj ʦaŋra tako~uwanan ato~u sasafaaj a wawa to~u fafaħijan naŋra ʦi no~ura, miriənaŋ to~u misakafaħaj a ɬ~ɮəmak.
S.TRANSL: They also encouraged me and their youngest daughter, Nora, to enroll as pioneers.

S.FORM: Oya tahalifaay to kafalic a tamdaw, ma'ilolay cangra to lipahak no i'ayaway a tayal.
Oya tahalifaay to kafalic a tamdaw, ma'ilolay cangra to lipahak no i'ayaway a tayal.
S.PHON: o~uja taħaɾifaaj to~u kafaɾiʦ a tamɬ~ɮaw, maʡiɾo~uɾaj ʦaŋra to~u ɾipaħak no~u iʡajawaj a tajaɾ.
S.TRANSL: Many of those going through such changes have admitted that they miss the satisfaction their work brought them.

S.FORM: Cowa ka padik ko sofitay no Loma i piloodan, samaanen mihatatanamay to romi'am'ad cangra to inaneng a misaka dademak to fonos.
Cowa ka padik ko sofitay no Loma i piloodan, samaanen mihatatanamay to romi'am'ad cangra to inaneng a misaka dademak to fonos.
S.PHON: ʦo~uwa ka paɬ~ɮik ko~u so~ufitaj no~u ɾo~uma i piɾo~uo~uɬ~ɮan, samaanən miħatatanamaj to~u ro~umiʡamʡaɬ~ɮ ʦaŋra to~u inanəŋ a misaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmak to~u fo~uno~us.
S.TRANSL: One reason that Roman soldiers were so effective is that they practiced with their weapons every day.

S.FORM: O 'alomanay cafacafay ni Kimy a micudad, cinalifetayto to madadi'iay a demak, nolatekay masasi pasengisengiay cangra.
O 'alomanay cafacafay ni Kimy a micudad, cinalifetayto to madadi'iay a demak, nolatekay masasi pasengisengiay cangra.
S.PHON: o~u ʡaɾo~umanaj ʦafaʦafaj ni kimj a miʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ, ʦinaɾifətajto~u to~u maɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮiʡiaj a ɬ~ɮəmak, no~uɾatəkaj masasi pasəŋisəŋiaj ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: Most of her classmates were sexually active, and after a typical weekend, they boasted of their latest sexual encounters.

S.FORM: Kawra, mikala cafayto ci Noma to kohecalay salikaka mitosil, halo tayra i loma' nangra ikor, lipahak pasowal cingra: "Mahecaday kitanan cangra, o kalo tamdaw cangra!"
Kawra, mikala cafayto ci Noma to kohecalay salikaka mitosil, halo tayra i loma' nangra ikor, lipahak pasowal cingra: "Mahecaday kitanan cangra, o kalo tamdaw cangra!"
S.PHON: kawra, mikaɾa ʦafajto~u ʦi no~uma to~u ko~uħəʦaɾaj saɾikaka mito~usiɾ, ħaɾo~u tajra i ɾo~umaʡ naŋra iko~ur, ɾipaħak paso~uwaɾ ʦiŋra: "maħəʦaɬ~ɮaj kitanan ʦaŋra, o~u kaɾo~u tamɬ~ɮaw ʦaŋra!"
S.TRANSL: But after sharing in the preaching work with the white Witnesses and enjoying hospitality in their homes, she exclaimed, "They are normal people just like us!"

S.FORM: Miliyasto cangra matawaay pasayni tamiyanan miwatawat to kamay!
Miliyasto cangra matawaay pasayni tamiyanan miwatawat to kamay!
S.PHON: miɾijasto~u ʦaŋra matawaaj pasajni tamijanan miwatawat to~u kamaj!
S.TRANSL: When they left, the chief smiled and waved to us.

S.FORM: Patireng cangra to patafangan cadada o sapasifana' to teloteloc a tamdaw, o madimadiay a sikawdan ni Yihofa cangra.
Patireng cangra to patafangan cadada o sapasifana' to teloteloc a tamdaw, o madimadiay a sikawdan ni Yihofa cangra.
S.PHON: patirəŋ ʦaŋra to~u patafaŋan ʦaɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮa o~u sapasifanaʡ to~u təɾo~utəɾo~uʦ a tamɬ~ɮaw, o~u maɬ~ɮimaɬ~ɮiaj a sikawɬ~ɮan ni jiħo~ufa ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: It was built so that in the future all would know that they too were faithful servants of Jehovah.

S.FORM: O wina ato lalimaay salikaka ni Yohani mihakelongay to Tingsokiw, misafelitay cangra to pihakelongan no wama.
O wina ato lalimaay salikaka ni Yohani mihakelongay to Tingsokiw, misafelitay cangra to pihakelongan no wama.
S.PHON: o~u wina ato~u ɾaɾimaaj saɾikaka ni jo~uħani miħakəɾo~uŋaj to~u tiŋso~ukiw, misafəɾitaj ʦaŋra to~u piħakəɾo~uŋan no~u wama.
S.TRANSL: John as well as his five brothers and sisters and their mother were all staunch Roman Catholics and as such were opposed to the father's faith.

S.FORM: Ma'araw no mita'lifay a tamdaw misa'icel kami tano mananamay a inaneng a misanga' to fadahong, masemeray cangra.
Ma'araw no mita'lifay a tamdaw misa'icel kami tano mananamay a inaneng a misanga' to fadahong, masemeray cangra.
S.PHON: maʡaraw no~u mitaʡɾifaj a tamɬ~ɮaw misaʡiʦəɾ kami tano~u mananamaj a inanəŋ a misaŋaʡ to~u faɬ~ɮaħo~uŋ, masəməraj ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: Seeing me struggle with this new trade was quite a spectacle for passersby.

S.FORM: So'lin mikitiniay cangra cowa ka hecad a kitakit, finacadan, sasowalen ato serangawan, kawra mitodongay cangra ci Yisan masasiolah.
So'lin mikitiniay cangra cowa ka hecad a kitakit, finacadan, sasowalen ato serangawan, kawra mitodongay cangra ci Yisan masasiolah.
S.PHON: so~uʡɾin mikitiniaj ʦaŋra ʦo~uwa ka ħəʦaɬ~ɮ a kitakit, finaʦaɬ~ɮan, saso~uwaɾən ato~u səraŋawan, kawra mito~uɬ~ɮo~uŋaj ʦaŋra ʦi jisan masasio~uɾaħ.
S.TRANSL: How grateful we are that Christlike love prevails among Jehovah's servants regardless of their nationality, tribe, language, or background!

S.FORM: Tano harateng ni Yihofa ko pinengneng ita to salikaka, mihaen cato ko hafay ko sapisawkit to samaanay a tamdaw cangra.
Tano harateng ni Yihofa ko pinengneng ita to salikaka, mihaen cato ko hafay ko sapisawkit to samaanay a tamdaw cangra.
S.PHON: tano~u ħaratəŋ ni jiħo~ufa ko~u pinəŋnəŋ ita to~u saɾikaka, miħaən ʦato~u ko~u ħafaj ko~u sapisawkit to~u samaanaj a tamɬ~ɮaw ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: Indeed, when we open our eyes and see our brothers as Jehovah sees them, we will not be tempted to judge them solely on what they have or do not have materially.

S.FORM: Ano 'orongen no wama wina ko wawa mirienang to cawma' i, nga'ayay fana'en ko wawa mipaca'i to mato'asay tamdaw, salakaten cangra a miala to kakaenen.
Ano 'orongen no wama wina ko wawa mirienang to cawma' i, nga'ayay fana'en ko wawa mipaca'i to mato'asay tamdaw, salakaten cangra a miala to kakaenen.
S.PHON: ano~u ʡo~uro~uŋən no~u wama wina ko~u wawa miriənaŋ to~u ʦawmaʡ i, ŋaʡajaj fanaʡən ko~u wawa mipaʦaʡi to~u mato~uʡasaj tamɬ~ɮaw, saɾakatən ʦaŋra a miaɾa to~u kakaənən.
S.TRANSL: At a social gathering, parents might tell their children to let older ones go ahead of them when lining up for food.

S.FORM: Cowa ka hecad ko Farisay tamdaw, cowa kakahi cangra paihekal to sida'it i tamdaw.
Cowa ka hecad ko Farisay tamdaw, cowa kakahi cangra paihekal to sida'it i tamdaw.
S.PHON: ʦo~uwa ka ħəʦaɬ~ɮ ko~u farisaj tamɬ~ɮaw, ʦo~uwa kakaħi ʦaŋra paiħəkaɾ to~u siɬ~ɮaʡit i tamɬ~ɮaw.
S.TRANSL: The Pharisees, in contrast, were reluctant to extend mercy.

S.FORM: Mirienang cangra i Iwropa, Asiya, Katimolay no Asiya a taykay, ikor tatayra i Hawaii Honololo, saikor tayra Karefoniya a Pasatina a mahererek.
Mirienang cangra i Iwropa, Asiya, Katimolay no Asiya a taykay, ikor tatayra i Hawaii Honololo, saikor tayra Karefoniya a Pasatina a mahererek.
S.PHON: miriənaŋ ʦaŋra i iwro~upa, asija, katimo~uɾaj no~u asija a tajkaj, iko~ur tatajra i ħawaii ħo~uno~uɾo~uɾo~u, saiko~ur tajra karəfo~unija a pasatina a maħərərək.
S.TRANSL: Included in the itinerary were stops in Lebanon and Jordan for special educational tours related to the lands of the Bible.

S.FORM: Mapalemeday cangra.
Mapalemeday cangra.
S.PHON: mapaɾəməɬ~ɮaj ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: This has led to rich blessings.

S.FORM: Tooren ko pinangan nangra, ano kinatolo mapakararaw ko tamdaw to malecaday a demak, cato ko sasododen cangra.
Tooren ko pinangan nangra, ano kinatolo mapakararaw ko tamdaw to malecaday a demak, cato ko sasododen cangra.
S.PHON: to~uo~urən ko~u pinaŋan naŋra, ano~u kinato~uɾo~u mapakararaw ko~u tamɬ~ɮaw to~u maɾəʦaɬ~ɮaj a ɬ~ɮəmak, ʦato~u ko~u saso~uɬ~ɮo~uɬ~ɮən ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: For example, according to tradition, they were unwilling to forgive the same offense more than three times.

S.FORM: Ikor, malalengo to ising cangra, licayan ko wina, makahi cingra malinah tayra i Matakaska hokira.
Ikor, malalengo to ising cangra, licayan ko wina, makahi cingra malinah tayra i Matakaska hokira.
S.PHON: iko~ur, maɾaɾəŋo~u to~u isiŋ ʦaŋra, ɾiʦajan ko~u wina, makaħi ʦiŋra maɾinaħ tajra i matakaska ħo~ukira.
S.TRANSL: After the couple had consulted Marie-Madeleine's doctor, they asked their mother to come and live with them in Madagascar.

S.FORM: Makalimay Cangra Mikinafalah, Itiniay i Tureky
Makalimay Cangra Mikinafalah, Itiniay i Tureky
S.PHON: makaɾimaj ʦaŋra mikinafaɾaħ, itiniaj i to~u~urəkj
S.TRANSL: They Offered Themselves Willingly, In Turkey

S.FORM: Tahira i kalahokan, micedengay cangra a romakat minokay, pito ko toki to papacem miliyaw a tayra mitosil.
Tahira i kalahokan, micedengay cangra a romakat minokay, pito ko toki to papacem miliyaw a tayra mitosil.
S.PHON: taħira i kaɾaħo~ukan, miʦəɬ~ɮəŋaj ʦaŋra a ro~umakat mino~ukaj, pito~u ko~u to~uki to~u papaʦəm miɾijaw a tajra mito~usiɾ.
S.TRANSL: The next morning, at seven o'clock, they are back in the square.

S.FORM: Kawra, hinatalaay cangra to sakaira no 'romi'ad ni Yihofa', mipala'afo toni tatiihay a hekal.
Kawra, hinatalaay cangra to sakaira no 'romi'ad ni Yihofa', mipala'afo toni tatiihay a hekal.
S.PHON: kawra, ħinataɾaaj ʦaŋra to~u sakaira no~u ʡro~umiʡaɬ~ɮ ni jiħo~ufaʡ, mipaɾaʡafo~u to~uni tatiiħaj a ħəkaɾ.
S.TRANSL: Rather, they await the end of this wicked system of things during "Jehovah's day."

S.FORM: Hay, samaanen ita mitodong cangra hani?
Hay, samaanen ita mitodong cangra hani?
S.PHON: ħaj, samaanən ita mito~uɬ~ɮo~uŋ ʦaŋra ħani?
S.TRANSL: In what practical ways, though, can we imitate their examples?

S.FORM: Caay ka pawan no Yutaya tamdaw konini, sapiliyawaw cangra mitengil to pitadtad ni Pawlo.
Caay ka pawan no Yutaya tamdaw konini, sapiliyawaw cangra mitengil to pitadtad ni Pawlo.
S.PHON: ʦaaj ka pawan no~u jo~u~utaja tamɬ~ɮaw ko~unini, sapiɾijawaw ʦaŋra mitəŋiɾ to~u pitaɬ~ɮtaɬ~ɮ ni pawɾo~u.
S.TRANSL: His argument impressed them, and they wanted to hear more.

S.FORM: O roma, papasifana' cangra to wawa, samaan patado to tao a miharateng.
O roma, papasifana' cangra to wawa, samaan patado to tao a miharateng.
S.PHON: o~u ro~uma, papasifanaʡ ʦaŋra to~u wawa, samaan pataɬ~ɮo~u to~u tao~u a miħaratəŋ.
S.TRANSL: Parents, of course, have the primary responsibility to teach their children how to be considerate of others.

S.FORM: Toha sapidamaaw to fadangalan a mitosil, marariday tayra i hoing cangra.
Toha sapidamaaw to fadangalan a mitosil, marariday tayra i hoing cangra.
S.PHON: to~uħa sapiɬ~ɮamaaw to~u faɬ~ɮaŋaɾan a mito~usiɾ, marariɬ~ɮaj tajra i ħo~uiŋ ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: To protect our right to do that, the brothers fought many legal battles.

S.FORM: 'Alomanay kasakilac kantok somowalay, maharek cangra ato fafahi miliso' to kasakopang i, macadaay ko miko'iday a tikami no salikaka, tada ta'angayay ko sapakalalok cangranan.
'Alomanay kasakilac kantok somowalay, maharek cangra ato fafahi miliso' to kasakopang i, macadaay ko miko'iday a tikami no salikaka, tada ta'angayay ko sapakalalok cangranan.
S.PHON: ʡaɾo~umanaj kasakiɾaʦ kanto~uk so~umo~uwaɾaj, maħarək ʦaŋra ato~u fafaħi miɾiso~uʡ to~u kasako~upaŋ i, maʦaɬ~ɮaaj ko~u miko~uʡiɬ~ɮaj a tikami no~u saɾikaka, taɬ~ɮa taʡaŋajaj ko~u sapakaɾaɾo~uk ʦaŋranan.
S.TRANSL: Many circuit overseers have stated how encouraged they and their wives felt after receiving even a short note of gratitude for their visit to a congregation.

S.FORM: Ano pakalaloken no wama wina ko wawa patireng to no'adingoan palinian, padama cangranan mitoor to palinian, o papatahekal to timolo cangra.
Ano pakalaloken no wama wina ko wawa patireng to no'adingoan palinian, padama cangranan mitoor to palinian, o papatahekal to timolo cangra.
S.PHON: ano~u pakaɾaɾo~ukən no~u wama wina ko~u wawa patirəŋ to~u no~uʡaɬ~ɮiŋo~uan paɾinian, paɬ~ɮama ʦaŋranan mito~uo~ur to~u paɾinian, o~u papataħəkaɾ to~u timo~uɾo~u ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: We show courage when we help our children to set and reach spiritual goals.

S.FORM: Samanen, o marariday cangra a miharateng to sakanga'ay no 'orip namo.
Samanen, o marariday cangra a miharateng to sakanga'ay no 'orip namo.
S.PHON: samanən, o~u marariɬ~ɮaj ʦaŋra a miħaratəŋ to~u sakaŋaʡaj no~u ʡo~urip namo~u.
S.TRANSL: After all, "they are keeping watch over you as those who will render an account."

S.FORM: Isaka 1963 miheca, maedeng 160 ko Misawacayay Tamdaw i Kirekistan, o 'alomanay naitiniay i Towic, Okeran, Rowasiya cangra.
Isaka 1963 miheca, maedeng 160 ko Misawacayay Tamdaw i Kirekistan, o 'alomanay naitiniay i Towic, Okeran, Rowasiya cangra.
S.PHON: isaka **** miħəʦa, maəɬ~ɮəŋ *** ko~u misawaʦajaj tamɬ~ɮaw i kirəkistan, o~u ʡaɾo~umanaj naitiniaj i to~uwiʦ, o~ukəran, ro~uwasija ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: By 1963 there were about 160 Witnesses in Kyrgyzstan, many of them originally from Germany, Ukraine, and Russia.

S.FORM: Mihayay cangra to cowa ka lefot ko tamdaw, mansa adihay ko katalawan a demak noniya hekal.
Mihayay cangra to cowa ka lefot ko tamdaw, mansa adihay ko katalawan a demak noniya hekal.
S.PHON: miħajaj ʦaŋra to~u ʦo~uwa ka ɾəfo~ut ko~u tamɬ~ɮaw, mansa aɬ~ɮiħaj ko~u kataɾawan a ɬ~ɮəmak no~unija ħəkaɾ.
S.TRANSL: They may recognize their own sinful state and the heartbreaking circumstances that have resulted from human sinfulness.

S.FORM: O roma miliyasay to so'linay kimad a tamdaw, samanen pasangaray cangra micada to pisalof no likakawa a mitadtad to sinael.
O roma miliyasay to so'linay kimad a tamdaw, samanen pasangaray cangra micada to pisalof no likakawa a mitadtad to sinael.
S.PHON: o~u ro~uma miɾijasaj to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ a tamɬ~ɮaw, samanən pasaŋaraj ʦaŋra miʦaɬ~ɮa to~u pisaɾo~uf no~u ɾikakawa a mitaɬ~ɮtaɬ~ɮ to~u sinaəɾ.
S.TRANSL: Some were stumbled by an adjusted understanding of a Bible passage or by what a prominent brother said or did.

S.FORM: Pakalaloken cangra patireng to no'adingoan a patosokan, hanaca padama cangranan mala'osi ko patosokan.
Pakalaloken cangra patireng to no'adingoan a patosokan, hanaca padama cangranan mala'osi ko patosokan.
S.PHON: pakaɾaɾo~ukən ʦaŋra patirəŋ to~u no~uʡaɬ~ɮiŋo~uan a pato~uso~ukan, ħanaʦa paɬ~ɮama ʦaŋranan maɾaʡo~usi ko~u pato~uso~ukan.
S.TRANSL: Encourage them to set specific spiritual goals, and help them to reach their goals.

S.FORM: 'Alomanay fa'inayan fafahiyan salikaka, na matiraay o mari'angay a oles, ikor masaheciay a mafalic cangra, rihadayto kala cafay to tao.
'Alomanay fa'inayan fafahiyan salikaka, na matiraay o mari'angay a oles, ikor masaheciay a mafalic cangra, rihadayto kala cafay to tao.
S.PHON: ʡaɾo~umanaj faʡinajan fafaħijan saɾikaka, na matiraaj o~u mariʡaŋaj a o~uɾəs, iko~ur masaħəʦiaj a mafaɾiʦ ʦaŋra, riħaɬ~ɮajto~u kaɾa ʦafaj to~u tao~u.
S.TRANSL: There are many who were once as fierce as wolves but who now live in peace with others.

S.FORM: Itiya, o KKK a mari'angay a kasakapot tada matenakay, miowingay cangra to misafinacaday a harateng, marariday ira ko mikari'angay a demak nangra.
Itiya, o KKK a mari'angay a kasakapot tada matenakay, miowingay cangra to misafinacaday a harateng, marariday ira ko mikari'angay a demak nangra.
S.PHON: itija, o~u kkk a mariʡaŋaj a kasakapo~ut taɬ~ɮa matənakaj, mio~uwiŋaj ʦaŋra to~u misafinaʦaɬ~ɮaj a ħaratəŋ, marariɬ~ɮaj ira ko~u mikariʡaŋaj a ɬ~ɮəmak naŋra.
S.TRANSL: The Ku Klux Klan KKK, an organization that promotes racism and violence, was very active then.

S.FORM: So'linay, 'alomanay salikaka misaaliay to patado ita cangranan a mitolon, wa mikitini cangra i hatiniay satolon milifok to haepoc.
So'linay, 'alomanay salikaka misaaliay to patado ita cangranan a mitolon, wa mikitini cangra i hatiniay satolon milifok to haepoc.
S.PHON: so~uʡɾinaj, ʡaɾo~umanaj saɾikaka misaaɾiaj to~u pataɬ~ɮo~u ita ʦaŋranan a mito~uɾo~un, wa mikitini ʦaŋra i ħatiniaj sato~uɾo~un miɾifo~uk to~u ħaəpo~uʦ.
S.TRANSL: Clearly, there are many brothers and sisters who need our prayers and who can benefit from them.

S.FORM: Nikawrira, mahecaday cangra kitanan, misa'icel palahad to mangodoay to Kawas a capeling, wa misaysayho to dangoc no niyah ato cowa kalefot a pakayraan.
Nikawrira, mahecaday cangra kitanan, misa'icel palahad to mangodoay to Kawas a capeling, wa misaysayho to dangoc no niyah ato cowa kalefot a pakayraan.
S.PHON: nikawrira, maħəʦaɬ~ɮaj ʦaŋra kitanan, misaʡiʦəɾ paɾaħaɬ~ɮ to~u maŋo~uɬ~ɮo~uaj to~u kawas a ʦapəɾiŋ, wa misajsajħo~u to~u ɬ~ɮaŋo~uʦ no~u nijaħ ato~u ʦo~uwa kaɾəfo~ut a pakajraan.
S.TRANSL: They, like us, have battled weaknesses and imperfections while trying to develop those godly qualities.

S.FORM: Kawra, hano makalim cangra miliyas to nalacolan hani?
Kawra, hano makalim cangra miliyas to nalacolan hani?
S.PHON: kawra, ħano~u makaɾim ʦaŋra miɾijas to~u naɾaʦo~uɾan ħani?
S.TRANSL: What caused them to leave their homeland?

S.FORM: Toya mihecaan ka 12 a folad, iraay ko Misawacayay Tamdaw ni Yihofa tayra i niyaro' niyam a mitosil, naitiniay i Angat cangra.
Toya mihecaan ka 12 a folad, iraay ko Misawacayay Tamdaw ni Yihofa tayra i niyaro' niyam a mitosil, naitiniay i Angat cangra.
S.PHON: to~uja miħəʦaan ka ** a fo~uɾaɬ~ɮ, iraaj ko~u misawaʦajaj tamɬ~ɮaw ni jiħo~ufa tajra i nijaro~uʡ nijam a mito~usiɾ, naitiniaj i aŋat ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: Shortly afterward, in December 1945, a group of Jehovah's Witnesses from the town of Angat came to preach in our village.

S.FORM: Ano tahalifa ko raramod to sapalifet i, wa misa'icel cangra misamaan to demak?
Ano tahalifa ko raramod to sapalifet i, wa misa'icel cangra misamaan to demak?
S.PHON: ano~u taħaɾifa ko~u raramo~uɬ~ɮ to~u sapaɾifət i, wa misaʡiʦəɾ ʦaŋra misamaan to~u ɬ~ɮəmak?
S.TRANSL: How does this apply if a mate is not a believer?

S.FORM: 'Alomanay ko mieminay to toki a mitosilay misawacayay cangra, ano mafalic ko pakayraan i, misarocod a mitosil wa padama cangraanan padadotoc mahemak ato tawlikay a harateng.
'Alomanay ko mieminay to toki a mitosilay misawacayay cangra, ano mafalic ko pakayraan i, misarocod a mitosil wa padama cangraanan padadotoc mahemak ato tawlikay a harateng.
S.PHON: ʡaɾo~umanaj ko~u miəminaj to~u to~uki a mito~usiɾaj misawaʦajaj ʦaŋra, ano~u mafaɾiʦ ko~u pakajraan i, misaro~uʦo~uɬ~ɮ a mito~usiɾ wa paɬ~ɮama ʦaŋraanan paɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮo~uto~uʦ maħəmak ato~u tawɾikaj a ħaratəŋ.
S.TRANSL: Many full-time servants can testify that focusing on the ministry helped them to maintain their balance despite changing circumstances.

S.FORM: Tiya malacafay cangra ci Yisan, mita'lif to Samariya i, sapikilimaw to pilafinan itira.
Tiya malacafay cangra ci Yisan, mita'lif to Samariya i, sapikilimaw to pilafinan itira.
S.PHON: tija maɾaʦafaj ʦaŋra ʦi jisan, mitaʡɾif to~u samarija i, sapikiɾimaw to~u piɾafinan itira.
S.TRANSL: When journeying with Jesus through Samaria, the disciples sought overnight lodging in a Samaritan village.

S.FORM: Sini'adaay to finawlan cingra, samanen "marawraw cangra a matiya o malafelay a siri to awaay ko pakaenay."
Sini'adaay to finawlan cingra, samanen "marawraw cangra a matiya o malafelay a siri to awaay ko pakaenay."
S.PHON: siniʡaɬ~ɮaaj to~u finawɾan ʦiŋra, samanən "marawraw ʦaŋra a matija o~u maɾafəɾaj a siri to~u awaaj ko~u pakaənaj."
S.TRANSL: He felt compassion for the crowds he met, for "they were skinned and thrown about like sheep without a shepherd."

S.FORM: Kawra, mamisinanot cangra to maanan a demak hani?
Kawra, mamisinanot cangra to maanan a demak hani?
S.PHON: kawra, mamisinano~ut ʦaŋra to~u maanan a ɬ~ɮəmak ħani?
S.TRANSL: What factors merit their consideration?

S.FORM: Pakahemeken cangra samanen, o sowal no matenesay a Krisciyang, pakalalok cangra wa matongal ko pakaso'lin.
Pakahemeken cangra samanen, o sowal no matenesay a Krisciyang, pakalalok cangra wa matongal ko pakaso'lin.
S.PHON: pakaħəməkən ʦaŋra samanən, o~u so~uwaɾ no~u matənəsaj a krisʦijaŋ, pakaɾaɾo~uk ʦaŋra wa mato~uŋaɾ ko~u pakaso~uʡɾin.
S.TRANSL: Commend them because approval, especially from a mature Christian, strengthens these young men and builds their confidence.

S.FORM: Isaka 2006 miheca, tayraayto i Matakaska cangra, maolahay to mitosilay demak itira.
Isaka 2006 miheca, tayraayto i Matakaska cangra, maolahay to mitosilay demak itira.
S.PHON: isaka **** miħəʦa, tajraajto~u i matakaska ʦaŋra, mao~uɾaħaj to~u mito~usiɾaj ɬ~ɮəmak itira.
S.TRANSL: They arrived in Madagascar in 2006 and enjoyed their ministry from the start.

S.FORM: Mankora, ano ma'araway nangra to marariday milawa kita to sa'opo, anca cowa ka hatira i 'ayaw a 'ifowa ko faloco' i, o tatangsol cangra mipadama kitaanan.
Mankora, ano ma'araway nangra to marariday milawa kita to sa'opo, anca cowa ka hatira i 'ayaw a 'ifowa ko faloco' i, o tatangsol cangra mipadama kitaanan.
S.PHON: manko~ura, ano~u maʡarawaj naŋra to~u marariɬ~ɮaj miɾawa kita to~u saʡo~upo~u, anʦa ʦo~uwa ka ħatira i ʡajaw a ʡifo~uwa ko~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ i, o~u tataŋso~uɾ ʦaŋra mipaɬ~ɮama kitaanan.
S.TRANSL: For instance, if they notice that we are missing meetings or that our zeal is cooling off, they will no doubt quickly come to our aid.

S.FORM: To sapaca'ofaw toni salicay, salakat mafana' kita to maanan ko lipahak hananay, ato misamaanay hano kamayan a lipahak ko romaroma tamdaw ano mapalifet cangra.
To sapaca'ofaw toni salicay, salakat mafana' kita to maanan ko lipahak hananay, ato misamaanay hano kamayan a lipahak ko romaroma tamdaw ano mapalifet cangra.
S.PHON: to~u sapaʦaʡo~ufaw to~uni saɾiʦaj, saɾakat mafanaʡ kita to~u maanan ko~u ɾipaħak ħananaj, ato~u misamaanaj ħano~u kamajan a ɾipaħak ko~u ro~umaro~uma tamɬ~ɮaw ano~u mapaɾifət ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: To answer that question, we first need to understand what genuine joy is and how others have remained joyful despite trials.

S.FORM: Padamaen Cangra Tanektek i Kimad
Padamaen Cangra Tanektek i Kimad
S.PHON: paɬ~ɮamaən ʦaŋra tanəktək i kimaɬ~ɮ
S.TRANSL: Teach Them to Stand Firm

S.FORM: Sowal no Fangcalay Cudad, o tamdaw itiya, "mafana'ay a midemak to tatiihay cangra," sanay.
Sowal no Fangcalay Cudad, o tamdaw itiya, "mafana'ay a midemak to tatiihay cangra," sanay.
S.PHON: so~uwaɾ no~u faŋʦaɾaj ʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ, o~u tamɬ~ɮaw itija, "mafanaʡaj a miɬ~ɮəmak to~u tatiiħaj ʦaŋra," sanaj.
S.TRANSL: In fact, the people had become "expert at doing what is bad."

S.FORM: Ikor no kalaholiw i Papilon to 70 a miheca, minokayayto tayni i Yirusalim cangra.
Ikor no kalaholiw i Papilon to 70 a miheca, minokayayto tayni i Yirusalim cangra.
S.PHON: iko~ur no~u kaɾaħo~uɾiw i papiɾo~un to~u ** a miħəʦa, mino~ukajajto~u tajni i jiro~u~usaɾim ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: After 70 long years of captivity, they were freed from Babylon.

S.FORM: 'Alomanay tamdaw 'ayaw no pinanam to Fangcalay Cudad, mahaenay o 'a'adopen a mari'ang ko pinangan, kawra ikor mafalicay cangra.
'Alomanay tamdaw 'ayaw no pinanam to Fangcalay Cudad, mahaenay o 'a'adopen a mari'ang ko pinangan, kawra ikor mafalicay cangra.
S.PHON: ʡaɾo~umanaj tamɬ~ɮaw ʡajaw no~u pinanam to~u faŋʦaɾaj ʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ, maħaənaj o~u ʡaʡaɬ~ɮo~upən a mariʡaŋ ko~u pinaŋan, kawra iko~ur mafaɾiʦaj ʦaŋra.
S.TRANSL: Many who formerly displayed such animalistic qualities have changed for the better.

S.FORM: Nga'ay cangra tano nowina sowal a mi'osi to Kimad no Kawas, tada kalipahakan a demak!
Nga'ay cangra tano nowina sowal a mi'osi to Kimad no Kawas, tada kalipahakan a demak!
S.PHON: ŋaʡaj ʦaŋra tano~u no~uwina so~uwaɾ a miʡo~usi to~u kimaɬ~ɮ no~u kawas, taɬ~ɮa kaɾipaħakan a ɬ~ɮəmak!
S.TRANSL: Just think how thrilling it must have been for them to hear or read God's Word in what had become their mother tongue!

S.FORM: Mahapinang no finawlan no Kawas ko saali no mitosilay a tayal, midemakay cangra to maanan?
Mahapinang no finawlan no Kawas ko saali no mitosilay a tayal, midemakay cangra to maanan?
S.PHON: maħapinaŋ no~u finawɾan no~u kawas ko~u saaɾi no~u mito~usiɾaj a tajaɾ, miɬ~ɮəmakaj ʦaŋra to~u maanan?
S.TRANSL: What kind of response to the preaching work is seen among Jehovah's people?

S.FORM: O aitira i loma' nangra konini a sapi'kak, ta matekop cangra a maemin," sanay.
O aitira i loma' nangra konini a sapi'kak, ta matekop cangra a maemin," sanay.
S.PHON: o~u aitira i ɾo~umaʡ naŋra ko~unini a sapiʡkak, ta matəko~up ʦaŋra a maəmin," sanaj.
S.TRANSL: Jehovah's adverse judgment cannot be kept out by bars and locks.

S.FORM: Misa'icel kita mifana' to tamdaw to nga'ayay ratoh, so'lin caka olah cangra i, wa malifokho ita ko lipahak.
Misa'icel kita mifana' to tamdaw to nga'ayay ratoh, so'lin caka olah cangra i, wa malifokho ita ko lipahak.
S.PHON: misaʡiʦəɾ kita mifanaʡ to~u tamɬ~ɮaw to~u ŋaʡajaj rato~uħ, so~uʡɾin ʦaka o~uɾaħ ʦaŋra i, wa maɾifo~ukħo~u ita ko~u ɾipaħak.
S.TRANSL: We can find happiness in giving people the opportunity to respond to the message of good news even if they choose not to listen.

S.FORM: 1944 mihecaan saka 6 folad cecay romi'ad, minokay kako micudad i, ma'araway ko cecay kihpicay cudad O Miliyaw Ira a Hekal, mitelian i 'eto.
1944 mihecaan saka 6 folad cecay romi'ad, minokay kako micudad i, ma'araway ko cecay kihpicay cudad O Miliyaw Ira a Hekal, mitelian i 'eto.
S.PHON: **** miħəʦaan saka * fo~uɾaɬ~ɮ ʦəʦaj ro~umiʡaɬ~ɮ, mino~ukaj kako~u miʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ i, maʡarawaj ko~u ʦəʦaj kiħpiʦaj ʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ o~u miɾijaw ira a ħəkaɾ, mitəɾian i ʡəto~u.
S.TRANSL: One day in June 1944 when I came home from school, I found a booklet entitled The Coming World Regeneration* on our dining-room table.

S.FORM: Maedeng toniya toki, o cecay Micekiway to Fangcalay Cudad ci Wiercilio Fukusen mihalakaay malinah tayra i Putawya, midama to mitosilay a demak.
Maedeng toniya toki, o cecay Micekiway to Fangcalay Cudad ci Wiercilio Fukusen mihalakaay malinah tayra i Putawya, midama to mitosilay a demak.
S.PHON: maəɬ~ɮəŋ to~unija to~uki, o~u ʦəʦaj miʦəkiwaj to~u faŋʦaɾaj ʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ ʦi wiərʦiɾio~u fo~u~uko~u~usən miħaɾakaaj maɾinaħ tajra i po~u~utawja, miɬ~ɮama to~u mito~usiɾaj a ɬ~ɮəmak.
S.TRANSL: About that time, Virgílio Ferguson, a Bible Student in Brazil, began making plans to move to Portugal to help with the Kingdom work.

S.FORM: 'Ayaw no pina patek miheca, cecay mitiliday to 'Olic mihayay, ano tahalifa ko tamdaw to pasangaray i, o hahalamham ci Yihofa.
'Ayaw no pina patek miheca, cecay mitiliday to 'Olic mihayay, ano tahalifa ko tamdaw to pasangaray i, o hahalamham ci Yihofa.
S.PHON: ʡajaw no~u pina patək miħəʦa, ʦəʦaj mitiɾiɬ~ɮaj to~u ʡo~uɾiʦ miħajaj, ano~u taħaɾifa ko~u tamɬ~ɮaw to~u pasaŋaraj i, o~u ħaħaɾamħam ʦi jiħo~ufa.
S.TRANSL: Centuries ago, a psalmist acknowledged the need for us to raise our eyes to Jehovah for help in times of need.

S.FORM: Haratengen ko cecay tinakoan, o cecay mihakelongay a Iciyopiya tamdaw, ikor no midoko to Kawas i Yirusalim i, malitemohay ci Filip i fatad no pinokayan lalan.
Haratengen ko cecay tinakoan, o cecay mihakelongay a Iciyopiya tamdaw, ikor no midoko to Kawas i Yirusalim i, malitemohay ci Filip i fatad no pinokayan lalan.
S.PHON: ħaratəŋən ko~u ʦəʦaj tinako~uan, o~u ʦəʦaj miħakəɾo~uŋaj a iʦijo~upija tamɬ~ɮaw, iko~ur no~u miɬ~ɮo~uko~u to~u kawas i jiro~u~usaɾim i, maɾitəmo~uħaj ʦi fiɾip i fataɬ~ɮ no~u pino~ukajan ɾaɾan.
S.TRANSL: Consider the example of the Ethiopian proselyte who was returning home after going to Jerusalem to worship.

S.FORM: Saka cecay, padadotocen ita micekiw a tangongol ko pidotim to so'linay kimad no Kawas.
Saka cecay, padadotocen ita micekiw a tangongol ko pidotim to so'linay kimad no Kawas.
S.PHON: saka ʦəʦaj, paɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮo~uto~uʦən ita miʦəkiw a taŋo~uŋo~uɾ ko~u piɬ~ɮo~utim to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ no~u kawas.
S.TRANSL: First, continue to study the precious truths of God's Word and meditate on them.

S.FORM: Itini saka cecay hahekalan, o roma kasakopang iraay ko mahaenay a pasangar, mansa palimo'oten ni Yakop ko Krisciyang misinanot toniya demak.
Itini saka cecay hahekalan, o roma kasakopang iraay ko mahaenay a pasangar, mansa palimo'oten ni Yakop ko Krisciyang misinanot toniya demak.
S.PHON: itini saka ʦəʦaj ħaħəkaɾan, o~u ro~uma kasako~upaŋ iraaj ko~u maħaənaj a pasaŋar, mansa paɾimo~uʡo~utən ni jako~up ko~u krisʦijaŋ misinano~ut to~unija ɬ~ɮəmak.
S.TRANSL: The disciple James warned that this problem was dividing some congregations in the first century.

S.FORM: Patinakoay ci Yis to cecay a caay ka moecel ko demak a matayalay.
Patinakoay ci Yis to cecay a caay ka moecel ko demak a matayalay.
S.PHON: patinako~uaj ʦi jis to~u ʦəʦaj a ʦaaj ka mo~uəʦəɾ ko~u ɬ~ɮəmak a matajaɾaj.
S.TRANSL: Jesus' illustration of the unrighteous steward is thought-provoking.

S.FORM: Ikor nora, pacekiwan ni Walte ko cecay 'a'ideman no dimeng.
Ikor nora, pacekiwan ni Walte ko cecay 'a'ideman no dimeng.
S.PHON: iko~ur no~ura, paʦəkiwan ni waɾtə ko~u ʦəʦaj ʡaʡiɬ~ɮəman no~u ɬ~ɮiməŋ.
S.TRANSL: Then Walter started a Bible study with the chief of police.

S.FORM: Ikor no sa'opo, o cecay mato'asay fafahiyan misakafahay mikilimay takowanan, cowa ko Misawacayay Tamdaw ko fa'inay ningra, cilosa'ay ko mata no salikaka a pasowal takowanan: "Misa'icelayto kako, awaayto ko pakayraan misaadihay to demak."

Ikor no sa'opo, o cecay mato'asay fafahiyan misakafahay mikilimay takowanan, cowa ko Misawacayay Tamdaw ko fa'inay ningra, cilosa'ay ko mata no salikaka a pasowal takowanan: "Misa'icelayto kako, awaayto ko pakayraan misaadihay to demak."
S.PHON: iko~ur no~u saʡo~upo~u, o~u ʦəʦaj mato~uʡasaj fafaħijan misakafaħaj mikiɾimaj tako~uwanan, ʦo~uwa ko~u misawaʦajaj tamɬ~ɮaw ko~u faʡinaj niŋra, ʦiɾo~usaʡaj ko~u mata no~u saɾikaka a paso~uwaɾ tako~uwanan: "misaʡiʦəɾajto~u kako~u, awaajto~u ko~u pakajraan misaaɬ~ɮiħaj to~u ɬ~ɮəmak."
S.TRANSL: After the meeting, an elderly pioneer sister, whose husband was not in the truth, said to me with tears in her eyes, "I tried, but I just couldn't do more than what I did."

S.FORM: Itini paro no loma' a picekiw, nga'ayay tooren iso kora ilaenoay sapasifana' a mihatatanam: Pili'en ko cecay kaolahan iso a radiw.
Itini paro no loma' a picekiw, nga'ayay tooren iso kora ilaenoay sapasifana' a mihatatanam: Pili'en ko cecay kaolahan iso a radiw.
S.PHON: itini paro~u no~u ɾo~umaʡ a piʦəkiw, ŋaʡajaj to~uo~urən iso~u ko~ura iɾaəno~uaj sapasifanaʡ a miħatatanam: piɾiʡən ko~u ʦəʦaj kao~uɾaħan iso~u a raɬ~ɮiw.
S.TRANSL: During family worship or even on your own, try the following: Choose one of your favorite songs from our songbook.

S.FORM: O cecay Yutaya a fa'inayan pakayni sapi'acaaw to so'liny kimad, misadakay to maanan sapi'aca aki?
O cecay Yutaya a fa'inayan pakayni sapi'acaaw to so'liny kimad, misadakay to maanan sapi'aca aki?
S.PHON: o~u ʦəʦaj jo~u~utaja a faʡinajan pakajni sapiʡaʦaaw to~u so~uʡɾinj kimaɬ~ɮ, misaɬ~ɮakaj to~u maanan sapiʡaʦa aki?
S.TRANSL: What price did one Jewish man pay for truth?

S.FORM: Itini i Karakas a Traris Prokeranto, o cecay wina ato wawa malacafay mitosil minanomay to salang no losay
Itini i Karakas a Traris Prokeranto, o cecay wina ato wawa malacafay mitosil minanomay to salang no losay
S.PHON: itini i karakas a traris pro~ukəranto~u, o~u ʦəʦaj wina ato~u wawa maɾaʦafaj mito~usiɾ minano~umaj to~u saɾaŋ no~u ɾo~usaj
S.TRANSL: A mother and her children enjoying some refreshment while sharing together in the ministry in Telares de Palo Grande, Caracas

S.FORM: O cecay kaying ci Mariko, tayraay cingra i Niwyok micudad.
O cecay kaying ci Mariko, tayraay cingra i Niwyok micudad.
S.PHON: o~u ʦəʦaj kajiŋ ʦi mariko~u, tajraaj ʦiŋra i niwjo~uk miʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Mariko,* a young Japanese woman, came to New York City, U.S.A., to attend school.

S.FORM: Sinanoten koni malifaay no cecay salikaka.
Sinanoten koni malifaay no cecay salikaka.
S.PHON: sinano~utən ko~uni maɾifaaj no~u ʦəʦaj saɾikaka.
S.TRANSL: Consider this real-life situation.

S.FORM: Saka cecay lalakaw wa milengo to kilang no fadisoso' ato mifolesakay to sapaloma tamdaw a sapatinako ni Yis, ato samaanay milingat to mitosilay demak koniya tosa sapatinako.
Saka cecay lalakaw wa milengo to kilang no fadisoso' ato mifolesakay to sapaloma tamdaw a sapatinako ni Yis, ato samaanay milingat to mitosilay demak koniya tosa sapatinako.
S.PHON: saka ʦəʦaj ɾaɾakaw wa miɾəŋo~u to~u kiɾaŋ no~u faɬ~ɮiso~uso~uʡ ato~u mifo~uɾəsakaj to~u sapaɾo~uma tamɬ~ɮaw a sapatinako~u ni jis, ato~u samaanaj miɾiŋat to~u mito~usiɾaj ɬ~ɮəmak ko~unija to~usa sapatinako~u.
S.TRANSL: The first of these two articles considers Jesus' parables of the vine and of the sower and explains what they teach us about our preaching work.

S.FORM: Saka cecay hahekalan iraay ko maanan tinakoan misawacayay, wa nga'ay maremo no tamdaw ko misakakinihay harateng a makelec malacecay?
Saka cecay hahekalan iraay ko maanan tinakoan misawacayay, wa nga'ay maremo no tamdaw ko misakakinihay harateng a makelec malacecay?
S.PHON: saka ʦəʦaj ħaħəkaɾan iraaj ko~u maanan tinako~uan misawaʦajaj, wa ŋaʡaj marəmo~u no~u tamɬ~ɮaw ko~u misakakiniħaj ħaratəŋ a makəɾəʦ maɾaʦəʦaj?
S.TRANSL: What first-century examples prove that people can overcome disunity?

S.FORM: Cecay laliyadan, ci Tafiti ato matayalay mato o 'adipel a midipot to pakaenay ni Napal ato siri ningra.
Cecay laliyadan, ci Tafiti ato matayalay mato o 'adipel a midipot to pakaenay ni Napal ato siri ningra.
S.PHON: ʦəʦaj ɾaɾijaɬ~ɮan, ʦi tafiti ato~u matajaɾaj mato~u o~u ʡaɬ~ɮipəɾ a miɬ~ɮipo~ut to~u pakaənaj ni napaɾ ato~u siri niŋra.
S.TRANSL: David and his men had been "like a protective wall" for Nabal's shepherds and flocks.

S.FORM: O cecay fafahiyan salikaka i Alapama no Padaka, mamipalahad to timoloay, honga' matoor ko no'adingoan a palinian ningra.
O cecay fafahiyan salikaka i Alapama no Padaka, mamipalahad to timoloay, honga' matoor ko no'adingoan a palinian ningra.
S.PHON: o~u ʦəʦaj fafaħijan saɾikaka i aɾapama no~u paɬ~ɮaka, mamipaɾaħaɬ~ɮ to~u timo~uɾo~uaj, ħo~uŋaʡ mato~uo~ur ko~u no~uʡaɬ~ɮiŋo~uan a paɾinian niŋra.
S.TRANSL: A Christian sister in Alabama, U.S.A., had to develop courage to set spiritual goals.

S.FORM: Tadtaden o cecay fafahiyan salikaka samanay milifok to sadama no kasakopang.
Tadtaden o cecay fafahiyan salikaka samanay milifok to sadama no kasakopang.
S.PHON: taɬ~ɮtaɬ~ɮən o~u ʦəʦaj fafaħijan saɾikaka samanaj miɾifo~uk to~u saɬ~ɮama no~u kasako~upaŋ.
S.TRANSL: Give an example of someone who was built up by a congregation's love.

S.FORM: Isaka 1987 miheca, papiliso'ay ko salikaka to cecay mahemekay to so'linay kimad a tamdaw i Palikeci.
Isaka 1987 miheca, papiliso'ay ko salikaka to cecay mahemekay to so'linay kimad a tamdaw i Palikeci.
S.PHON: isaka **** miħəʦa, papiɾiso~uʡaj ko~u saɾikaka to~u ʦəʦaj maħəməkaj to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ a tamɬ~ɮaw i paɾikəʦi.
S.TRANSL: In 1987 a brother asked me to visit an interested person living in the town of Balykchy.

S.FORM: O cecay kapah salikaka sanay ko sowal: "Nga'ay ko pisaka dademak to pasalat i kaemangan, ano maraod ko pisa'osian to kalimlaan a demak i, wa ira ko sapadama to niyah, tinakoen mikilim to matatodongay hatayal, anca misa'osi to caciraramod anca padotoc a caka ciraramod to cecay liyad."

O cecay kapah salikaka sanay ko sowal: "Nga'ay ko pisaka dademak to pasalat i kaemangan, ano maraod ko pisa'osian to kalimlaan a demak i, wa ira ko sapadama to niyah, tinakoen mikilim to matatodongay hatayal, anca misa'osi to caciraramod anca padotoc a caka ciraramod to cecay liyad."
S.PHON: o~u ʦəʦaj kapaħ saɾikaka sanaj ko~u so~uwaɾ: "ŋaʡaj ko~u pisaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmak to~u pasaɾat i kaəmaŋan, ano~u marao~uɬ~ɮ ko~u pisaʡo~usian to~u kaɾimɾaan a ɬ~ɮəmak i, wa ira ko~u sapaɬ~ɮama to~u nijaħ, tinako~uən mikiɾim to~u matato~uɬ~ɮo~uŋaj ħatajaɾ, anʦa misaʡo~usi to~u ʦaʦiraramo~uɬ~ɮ anʦa paɬ~ɮo~uto~uʦ a ʦaka ʦiraramo~uɬ~ɮ to~u ʦəʦaj ɾijaɬ~ɮ."
S.TRANSL: One young brother said: "Using freedom wisely as a young person really helps when later you are faced with bigger decisions, such as finding a suitable job or choosing whether to marry or to remain single for a time."

S.FORM: Saka cecay lalakaw mitadtaday, hato matatodongay ko harateng no hekal tamdaw ano saan kita, wa samanen ko demak no mita.
Saka cecay lalakaw mitadtaday, hato matatodongay ko harateng no hekal tamdaw ano saan kita, wa samanen ko demak no mita.
S.PHON: saka ʦəʦaj ɾaɾakaw mitaɬ~ɮtaɬ~ɮaj, ħato~u matato~uɬ~ɮo~uŋaj ko~u ħaratəŋ no~u ħəkaɾ tamɬ~ɮaw ano~u saan kita, wa samanən ko~u ɬ~ɮəmak no~u mita.
S.TRANSL: The first article explains what to do when we hear worldly ideas that may sound appealing or convincing.

S.FORM: Mankora caka pakaala to cecay miheca, papiliyasan no wina kako tora picudadan.
Mankora caka pakaala to cecay miheca, papiliyasan no wina kako tora picudadan.
S.PHON: manko~ura ʦaka pakaaɾa to~u ʦəʦaj miħəʦa, papiɾijasan no~u wina kako~u to~ura piʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮan.
S.TRANSL: So my mother allowed me to leave the school within a year.

S.FORM: Misadolo to noka'orip a cecay pakayraan i, isalakaten micangacang ko Hongti Kitakit no Kawas.
Misadolo to noka'orip a cecay pakayraan i, isalakaten micangacang ko Hongti Kitakit no Kawas.
S.PHON: misaɬ~ɮo~uɾo~u to~u no~ukaʡo~urip a ʦəʦaj pakajraan i, isaɾakatən miʦaŋaʦaŋ ko~u ħo~uŋti kitakit no~u kawas.
S.TRANSL: Keeping our life simple involves setting priorities.

S.FORM: Iraay ko cecay dafong miki'ayaway to misanga'an no tamdaw, pakidemay ko pidoka' to tamdaw, o misamangahay!
Iraay ko cecay dafong miki'ayaway to misanga'an no tamdaw, pakidemay ko pidoka' to tamdaw, o misamangahay!
S.PHON: iraaj ko~u ʦəʦaj ɬ~ɮafo~uŋ mikiʡajawaj to~u misaŋaʡan no~u tamɬ~ɮaw, pakiɬ~ɮəmaj ko~u piɬ~ɮo~ukaʡ to~u tamɬ~ɮaw, o~u misamaŋaħaj!
S.TRANSL: However, there is a device that predates all of them and that has had the worst possible effect on humankind. What is it?

S.FORM: O cecay malaplapay tayra i Sipoliya i, o ci Emil Yancen, isaka 1919 miheca a hofocen cingra.
O cecay malaplapay tayra i Sipoliya i, o ci Emil Yancen, isaka 1919 miheca a hofocen cingra.
S.PHON: o~u ʦəʦaj maɾapɾapaj tajra i sipo~uɾija i, o~u ʦi əmiɾ janʦən, isaka **** miħəʦa a ħo~ufo~uʦən ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: One such former exile was Emil Yantzen, who had been born in Kyrgyzstan in 1919.

S.FORM: Nolatekay, cecay tamdaw ano iraay ko tadamaanay a kafana'an ato nalifetan, kawra iraay ko roma saali to cowa ko kananaman nangra a tayal.
Nolatekay, cecay tamdaw ano iraay ko tadamaanay a kafana'an ato nalifetan, kawra iraay ko roma saali to cowa ko kananaman nangra a tayal.
S.PHON: no~uɾatəkaj, ʦəʦaj tamɬ~ɮaw ano~u iraaj ko~u taɬ~ɮamaanaj a kafanaʡan ato~u naɾifətan, kawra iraaj ko~u ro~uma saaɾi to~u ʦo~uwa ko~u kananaman naŋra a tajaɾ.
S.TRANSL: Yet, Jehovah appreciates the self-sacrificing spirit shown by individuals who offer themselves to serve wherever there is a need.

S.FORM: Oraanto, o cecay kohecalay a salikaka mitooray micomod i kamaisi'an, ikakaay ko nika takaraw ningra i takowanan atoya mari'angay fa'inayan.
Oraanto, o cecay kohecalay a salikaka mitooray micomod i kamaisi'an, ikakaay ko nika takaraw ningra i takowanan atoya mari'angay fa'inayan.
S.PHON: o~uraanto~u, o~u ʦəʦaj ko~uħəʦaɾaj a saɾikaka mito~uo~uraj miʦo~umo~uɬ~ɮ i kamaisiʡan, ikakaaj ko~u nika takaraw niŋra i tako~uwanan ato~uja mariʡaŋaj faʡinajan.
S.TRANSL: But a white brother, far larger than either me or the rough-looking customer, came in after us.

S.FORM: Patinakoen, ya miliso' kami to cecay niyaro' i Tinasi i, malitemohay ko pirarakat i lalan no KKK a kasakapot.
Patinakoen, ya miliso' kami to cecay niyaro' i Tinasi i, malitemohay ko pirarakat i lalan no KKK a kasakapot.
S.PHON: patinako~uən, ja miɾiso~uʡ kami to~u ʦəʦaj nijaro~uʡ i tinasi i, maɾitəmo~uħaj ko~u pirarakat i ɾaɾan no~u kkk a kasakapo~ut.
S.TRANSL: One time, the KKK held a march in a town we were visiting in Tennessee.

S.FORM: Kaemangaho kako, patadoay maomah ko wama to cecay tawki, mietan to saka'orip.
Kaemangaho kako, patadoay maomah ko wama to cecay tawki, mietan to saka'orip.
S.PHON: kaəmaŋaħo~u kako~u, pataɬ~ɮo~uaj mao~umaħ ko~u wama to~u ʦəʦaj tawki, miətan to~u sakaʡo~urip.
S.TRANSL: When I was born, my father was working for one of the local farmers.

S.FORM: Ano kaolahan no 'alomanay ko cecay faelohay dafong, wa adihay ko misanga'an.
Ano kaolahan no 'alomanay ko cecay faelohay dafong, wa adihay ko misanga'an.
S.PHON: ano~u kao~uɾaħan no~u ʡaɾo~umanaj ko~u ʦəʦaj faəɾo~uħaj ɬ~ɮafo~uŋ, wa aɬ~ɮiħaj ko~u misaŋaʡan.
S.TRANSL: When a new invention or innovation becomes popular, it is eventually mass-produced.

S.FORM: Saka cecay, cowa pisakakinih ko faloco' ni Yis to tamdaw.
Saka cecay, cowa pisakakinih ko faloco' ni Yis to tamdaw.
S.PHON: saka ʦəʦaj, ʦo~uwa pisakakiniħ ko~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ ni jis to~u tamɬ~ɮaw.
S.TRANSL: First, he rejected prejudice, being totally impartial.

S.FORM: O cecay paro no loma' wa misinanot misaka dademak to payci, pakayni saka dademakaw ci Yihofaan wa midolo noka'orip, milowan to mapalakay a payci.
O cecay paro no loma' wa misinanot misaka dademak to payci, pakayni saka dademakaw ci Yihofaan wa midolo noka'orip, milowan to mapalakay a payci.
S.PHON: o~u ʦəʦaj paro~u no~u ɾo~umaʡ wa misinano~ut misaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmak to~u pajʦi, pakajni saka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmakaw ʦi jiħo~ufaan wa miɬ~ɮo~uɾo~u no~ukaʡo~urip, miɾo~uwan to~u mapaɾakaj a pajʦi.
S.TRANSL: A family may make adjustments as they seek to balance income with expenses or as they consider how to simplify their life and reduce expenses in order to do more for Jehovah.

S.FORM: Isaka 2011 miheca, iraay ko 'atoray ato conami i Dipong, o cecay fafahiyan salikaka ta'angayay ko nika lepat no loma'.
Isaka 2011 miheca, iraay ko 'atoray ato conami i Dipong, o cecay fafahiyan salikaka ta'angayay ko nika lepat no loma'.
S.PHON: isaka **** miħəʦa, iraaj ko~u ʡato~uraj ato~u ʦo~unami i ɬ~ɮipo~uŋ, o~u ʦəʦaj fafaħijan saɾikaka taʡaŋajaj ko~u nika ɾəpat no~u ɾo~umaʡ.
S.TRANSL: One Japanese sister lived in an area that was seriously damaged by the earthquake and tsunami of 2011.

S.FORM: Iraay ko cecay mangangahay fa'inayan salikaka, micada tamiyanan atoya sikawdan no fenpo no Ingkelan ci Polays Hiws.

S.TRANSL: How pleased Jehovah will be as he observes our whole-souled efforts to "keep bearing much fruit"!

S.FORM: Maacekay ko 'alomanay tamdaw cingranan, matalaway cingra ngataen no tamdaw.
Maacekay ko 'alomanay tamdaw cingranan, matalaway cingra ngataen no tamdaw.
S.PHON: maaʦəkaj ko~u ʡaɾo~umanaj tamɬ~ɮaw ʦiŋranan, mataɾawaj ʦiŋra ŋataən no~u tamɬ~ɮaw.
S.TRANSL: He was obnoxious and had a knack for keeping people away from him.

S.FORM: Mihaenay, pasangaray a mirienang cingra to sa'opo.
Mihaenay, pasangaray a mirienang cingra to sa'opo.
S.PHON: miħaənaj, pasaŋaraj a miriənaŋ ʦiŋra to~u saʡo~upo~u.
S.TRANSL: Understandably, she has sometimes found it difficult to attend congregation meetings.

S.FORM: Mahemekay Cingra pasadak to tefoc,
Mahemekay Cingra pasadak to tefoc,
S.PHON: maħəməkaj ʦiŋra pasaɬ~ɮak to~u təfo~uʦ,
S.TRANSL: If He did not withhold the gift of His dear Son,

S.FORM: Samaanan ni Yihofa patalahekal to mafana'ay Cingra tora pasangar ningra hani?
Samaanan ni Yihofa patalahekal to mafana'ay Cingra tora pasangar ningra hani?
S.PHON: samaanan ni jiħo~ufa pataɾaħəkaɾ to~u mafanaʡaj ʦiŋra to~ura pasaŋar niŋra ħani?
S.TRANSL: How did Jehovah show his understanding of her problems?

S.FORM: Sowal ni Tafiti: 'Pakiyadi han cingra a mapo'i.'
Sowal ni Tafiti: 'Pakiyadi han cingra a mapo'i.'
S.PHON: so~uwaɾ ni tafiti: ʡpakijaɬ~ɮi ħan ʦiŋra a mapo~uʡi.ʡ
S.TRANSL: David said: "Leave him alone!"

S.FORM: Iraay ko lalangian ni Marta a paso'lin, oya madimadiay a salikaka ningra ci Lacarus, "o lalomowad cingra a ma'orip nai patay i saikoray a romi'ad."
Iraay ko lalangian ni Marta a paso'lin, oya madimadiay a salikaka ningra ci Lacarus, "o lalomowad cingra a ma'orip nai patay i saikoray a romi'ad."
S.PHON: iraaj ko~u ɾaɾaŋian ni marta a paso~uʡɾin, o~uja maɬ~ɮimaɬ~ɮiaj a saɾikaka niŋra ʦi ɾaʦaro~u~us, "o~u ɾaɾo~umo~uwaɬ~ɮ ʦiŋra a maʡo~urip nai pataj i saiko~uraj a ro~umiʡaɬ~ɮ."
S.TRANSL: Martha clearly had reason to be confident that her faithful brother, Lazarus, would "rise in the resurrection on the last day."

S.FORM: O Misawacayay Tamdaw ni Yihofa cingra.
O Misawacayay Tamdaw ni Yihofa cingra.
S.PHON: o~u misawaʦajaj tamɬ~ɮaw ni jiħo~ufa ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: She noticed John's injured finger and offered to help.

S.FORM: Maharateng noya mitiliday to 'Olic a tamdaw, to papatikoren a patireng ko Yirusalim i, hemek sa romadiw: "Cingra ko patanektekay to 'adawang no niyaro' iso, Cingra ko palemeday to wawa iso.
Maharateng noya mitiliday to 'Olic a tamdaw, to papatikoren a patireng ko Yirusalim i, hemek sa romadiw: "Cingra ko patanektekay to 'adawang no niyaro' iso, Cingra ko palemeday to wawa iso.
S.PHON: maħaratəŋ no~uja mitiɾiɬ~ɮaj to~u ʡo~uɾiʦ a tamɬ~ɮaw, to~u papatiko~urən a patirəŋ ko~u jiro~u~usaɾim i, ħəmək sa ro~umaɬ~ɮiw: "ʦiŋra ko~u patanəktəkaj to~u ʡaɬ~ɮawaŋ no~u nijaro~uʡ iso~u, ʦiŋra ko~u paɾəməɬ~ɮaj to~u wawa iso~u.
S.TRANSL: In thinking about the restoration of Jerusalem, the psalmist sang about Jehovah: "He makes the bars of your city gates strong; he blesses your sons within you.

S.FORM: Cihafayay ko loma' ni Yopu, ato cinganganay cingra, hanaca o tada kangodoan no tamdaw cingra.
Cihafayay ko loma' ni Yopu, ato cinganganay cingra, hanaca o tada kangodoan no tamdaw cingra.
S.PHON: ʦiħafajaj ko~u ɾo~umaʡ ni jo~upo~u~u, ato~u ʦiŋaŋanaj ʦiŋra, ħanaʦa o~u taɬ~ɮa kaŋo~uɬ~ɮo~uan no~u tamɬ~ɮaw ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: He was wealthy, well-known, and highly respected.

S.FORM: Mihatatanamay ci Riana to Tantelo a sowal, samanen mafana'ay cingra, iraayho ko 'alomanay cowa ka matengil to lihaf no Hongti Kitakit.
Mihatatanamay ci Riana to Tantelo a sowal, samanen mafana'ay cingra, iraayho ko 'alomanay cowa ka matengil to lihaf no Hongti Kitakit.
S.PHON: miħatatanamaj ʦi riana to~u tantəɾo~u a so~uwaɾ, samanən mafanaʡaj ʦiŋra, iraajħo~u ko~u ʡaɾo~umanaj ʦo~uwa ka matəŋiɾ to~u ɾiħaf no~u ħo~uŋti kitakit.
S.TRANSL: He learned some expressions in their language and thought about the many Antandroy people who had not yet been reached with the Kingdom message.

S.FORM: Ci Yihofa ko misanga'ay kitanan, edeng Cingra ko mamisa'osi to nga'ayay ato tatiihay palinian.
Ci Yihofa ko misanga'ay kitanan, edeng Cingra ko mamisa'osi to nga'ayay ato tatiihay palinian.
S.PHON: ʦi jiħo~ufa ko~u misaŋaʡaj kitanan, əɬ~ɮəŋ ʦiŋra ko~u mamisaʡo~usi to~u ŋaʡajaj ato~u tatiiħaj paɾinian.
S.TRANSL: Jehovah has the right to make laws for us because he created us.

S.FORM: Ikorto cowato ka nga'ay ci Taniayer padotoc a miemin to toki a matayal, kamayan cingra a midama takowanan.
Ikorto cowato ka nga'ay ci Taniayer padotoc a miemin to toki a matayal, kamayan cingra a midama takowanan.
S.PHON: iko~urto~u ʦo~uwato~u ka ŋaʡaj ʦi taniajər paɬ~ɮo~uto~uʦ a miəmin to~u to~uki a matajaɾ, kamajan ʦiŋra a miɬ~ɮama tako~uwanan.
S.TRANSL: When Danièle was no longer able to work full-time, she made it a point to support me in more ways.

S.FORM: Mirienangay cingra to "pararamoday," ato "tadamaanay a sapataheka."
Mirienangay cingra to "pararamoday," ato "tadamaanay a sapataheka."
S.PHON: miriənaŋaj ʦiŋra to~u "pararamo~uɬ~ɮaj," ato~u "taɬ~ɮamaanaj a sapataħəka."
S.TRANSL: He attended "a marriage feast" and "a big reception feast."

S.FORM: Tiya cato paka' met ci Yosif to 'iwil no faloco' ika'ayaw no kaka i, misamaanay cingra?
Tiya cato paka' met ci Yosif to 'iwil no faloco' ika'ayaw no kaka i, misamaanay cingra?
S.PHON: tija ʦato~u pakaʡ mət ʦi jo~usif to~u ʡiwiɾ no~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ ikaʡajaw no~u kaka i, misamaanaj ʦiŋra?
S.TRANSL: How did Joseph restrain his emotions in dealing with his brothers?

S.FORM: O fafahi ningra cowa ko Misawacayay Tamdaw ni Yihofa, saka pacekiwan ni Koloriya cingra.
O fafahi ningra cowa ko Misawacayay Tamdaw ni Yihofa, saka pacekiwan ni Koloriya cingra.
S.PHON: o~u fafaħi niŋra ʦo~uwa ko~u misawaʦajaj tamɬ~ɮaw ni jiħo~ufa, saka paʦəkiwan ni ko~uɾo~urija ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: His wife was then an unbeliever, and Gloria started a Bible study with her.

S.FORM: Ora coko padetengay a mipatayay o nganga'ay milifok to sapadama, kawra oha mamiki'ayaw cingra misaali to sapadama, halo micada toni limasmas.
Ora coko padetengay a mipatayay o nganga'ay milifok to sapadama, kawra oha mamiki'ayaw cingra misaali to sapadama, halo micada toni limasmas.
S.PHON: o~ura ʦo~uko~u paɬ~ɮətəŋaj a mipatajaj o~u ŋaŋaʡaj miɾifo~uk to~u sapaɬ~ɮama, kawra o~uħa mamikiʡajaw ʦiŋra misaaɾi to~u sapaɬ~ɮama, ħaɾo~u miʦaɬ~ɮa to~uni ɾimasmas.
S.TRANSL: Help was available to the unintentional manslayer, but he had to seek out and accept that help.

S.FORM: MA'ARAWTO ni Cikariya ko saka 7 a mapolosi ikor, misimsimay to adihayay demak cingra.
MA'ARAWTO ni Cikariya ko saka 7 a mapolosi ikor, misimsimay to adihayay demak cingra.
S.PHON: maʡarawto~u ni ʦikarija ko~u saka * a mapo~uɾo~usi iko~ur, misimsimaj to~u aɬ~ɮiħajaj ɬ~ɮəmak ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: AS Zechariah's seventh vision closes, the prophet has much to think about.

S.FORM: So'lin macada ni Kimmi ko mari'angay, sini'adaayho cingra to mitosilan a tamdaw, samaanay ko piremo ningra to pidemak hani?
So'lin macada ni Kimmi ko mari'angay, sini'adaayho cingra to mitosilan a tamdaw, samaanay ko piremo ningra to pidemak hani?
S.PHON: so~uʡɾin maʦaɬ~ɮa ni kimmi ko~u mariʡaŋaj, siniʡaɬ~ɮaajħo~u ʦiŋra to~u mito~usiɾan a tamɬ~ɮaw, samaanaj ko~u pirəmo~u niŋra to~u piɬ~ɮəmak ħani?
S.TRANSL: How does Kim maintain her compassion despite such treatment?

S.FORM: Ano awaay ko timolo ni Solomon, wa sawad sato cingra to nika talaw, ikaka ko nika tatiih to caka laheci masanga' ko sefi.
Ano awaay ko timolo ni Solomon, wa sawad sato cingra to nika talaw, ikaka ko nika tatiih to caka laheci masanga' ko sefi.
S.PHON: ano~u awaaj ko~u timo~uɾo~u ni so~uɾo~umo~un, wa sawaɬ~ɮ sato~u ʦiŋra to~u nika taɾaw, ikaka ko~u nika tatiiħ to~u ʦaka ɾaħəʦi masaŋaʡ ko~u səfi.
S.TRANSL: If Solomon did not prove to be courageous, fear could paralyze him, and inactivity would be worse than failure.

S.FORM: Ano caka tayra cingra milaliw i pilimekan a niyaro' i, wa ira ko fadangalan no malininaay nora mapatayay a mipatay cingranan.
Ano caka tayra cingra milaliw i pilimekan a niyaro' i, wa ira ko fadangalan no malininaay nora mapatayay a mipatay cingranan.
S.PHON: ano~u ʦaka tajra ʦiŋra miɾaɾiw i piɾiməkan a nijaro~uʡ i, wa ira ko~u faɬ~ɮaŋaɾan no~u maɾininaaj no~ura mapatajaj a mipataj ʦiŋranan.
S.TRANSL: If he did not seek refuge in one of the cities Jehovah had set aside, the closest relative of the person he had killed was free to put him to death.

S.FORM: Saliyaliyawsa ci Komil ma'a'odang to tao, kawra misododan ni Hosiya cingra a miliyaw madadi'i.
Saliyaliyawsa ci Komil ma'a'odang to tao, kawra misododan ni Hosiya cingra a miliyaw madadi'i.
S.PHON: saɾijaɾijawsa ʦi ko~umiɾ maʡaʡo~uɬ~ɮaŋ to~u tao~u, kawra miso~uɬ~ɮo~uɬ~ɮan ni ħo~usija ʦiŋra a miɾijaw maɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮiʡi.
S.TRANSL: If a Christian's mate commits sexual immorality, the innocent mate can extend forgiveness.

S.FORM: Cowa ka lecad ci Yihofa to tamdaw, ano saliyaliyaw han ita mingitangit Cingra, cowa ko mamaesam Cingra.
Cowa ka lecad ci Yihofa to tamdaw, ano saliyaliyaw han ita mingitangit Cingra, cowa ko mamaesam Cingra.
S.PHON: ʦo~uwa ka ɾəʦaɬ~ɮ ʦi jiħo~ufa to~u tamɬ~ɮaw, ano~u saɾijaɾijaw ħan ita miŋitaŋit ʦiŋra, ʦo~uwa ko~u mamaəsam ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: Unlike human counselors, he never gets tired of hearing our petitions, even when we ask for his help over and over.

S.FORM: Hay, mamisamaan Cingra a padama kitanan hani?
Hay, mamisamaan Cingra a padama kitanan hani?
S.PHON: ħaj, mamisamaan ʦiŋra a paɬ~ɮama kitanan ħani?
S.TRANSL: But how does he do so?

S.FORM: Marariday tafihaw ko faloco' a mato'olen cingra.
Marariday tafihaw ko faloco' a mato'olen cingra.
S.PHON: marariɬ~ɮaj tafiħaw ko~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ a mato~uʡo~uɾən ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: Growing up, she struggled with loneliness and a feeling of emptiness.

S.FORM: To nika awa no nilifetan, cato pakafilo toni 'inorong cingra han?
To nika awa no nilifetan, cato pakafilo toni 'inorong cingra han?
S.PHON: to~u nika awa no~u niɾifətan, ʦato~u pakafiɾo~u to~uni ʡino~uro~uŋ ʦiŋra ħan?
S.TRANSL: Would his youth and inexperience be an obstacle?

S.FORM: Iraayto ko Kihecod a Dafong ni Yihofa Hano Patelongenho to Dafong Cingra Hani?
Iraayto ko Kihecod a Dafong ni Yihofa Hano Patelongenho to Dafong Cingra Hani?
S.PHON: iraajto~u ko~u kiħəʦo~uɬ~ɮ a ɬ~ɮafo~uŋ ni jiħo~ufa ħano~u patəɾo~uŋənħo~u to~u ɬ~ɮafo~uŋ ʦiŋra ħani?
S.TRANSL: Why Give to the One Who Has Everything?

S.FORM: Sanay cingra: "Palidlid sanay kako mapawan to no'adingoan a patosokan.
Sanay cingra: "Palidlid sanay kako mapawan to no'adingoan a patosokan.
S.PHON: sanaj ʦiŋra: "paɾiɬ~ɮɾiɬ~ɮ sanaj kako~u mapawan to~u no~uʡaɬ~ɮiŋo~uan a pato~uso~ukan.
S.TRANSL: She says: "My spiritual goals started to fade.

S.FORM: Misaka dademakay cingra to safalod i Taneng 20:5, mitala to matatodongay a toki 'pakapipatalahkal to harateng' no mako, kawrira cowa koyaan hahimeden a demak."
Misaka dademakay cingra to safalod i Taneng 20:5, mitala to matatodongay a toki 'pakapipatalahkal to harateng' no mako, kawrira cowa koyaan hahimeden a demak."
S.PHON: misaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmakaj ʦiŋra to~u safaɾo~uɬ~ɮ i tanəŋ **:*, mitaɾa to~u matato~uɬ~ɮo~uŋaj a to~uki ʡpakapipataɾaħkaɾ to~u ħaratəŋʡ no~u mako~u, kawrira ʦo~uwa ko~ujaan ħaħiməɬ~ɮən a ɬ~ɮəmak."
S.TRANSL: "Then she will apply the principle found at Proverbs 20:5, even when this means waiting for the right time to 'draw out' my thoughts if it is a matter that I am free to discuss with her."

S.FORM: Caka halafin, cingra ato fafahi ci Lici tayniay i Putawya, miliyaw malacafay ci Yangkean.
Caka halafin, cingra ato fafahi ci Lici tayniay i Putawya, miliyaw malacafay ci Yangkean.
S.PHON: ʦaka ħaɾafin, ʦiŋra ato~u fafaħi ʦi ɾiʦi tajniaj i po~u~utawja, miɾijaw maɾaʦafaj ʦi jaŋkəan.
S.TRANSL: Before long, Virgílio set sail with his wife, Lizzie, to join Brother Young again.

S.FORM: Isatapang mararomay cingra ato fafahi ningra, o sowal ningra: "Tada misalimlaay kami toniya tayal, hanaca iraayho ko 'icel no niyah padotoc a mitayal."
Isatapang mararomay cingra ato fafahi ningra, o sowal ningra: "Tada misalimlaay kami toniya tayal, hanaca iraayho ko 'icel no niyah padotoc a mitayal."
S.PHON: isatapaŋ mararo~umaj ʦiŋra ato~u fafaħi niŋra, o~u so~uwaɾ niŋra: "taɬ~ɮa misaɾimɾaaj kami to~unija tajaɾ, ħanaʦa iraajħo~u ko~u ʡiʦəɾ no~u nijaħ paɬ~ɮo~uto~uʦ a mitajaɾ."
S.TRANSL: "We appreciated our privilege very much and felt that we had the stamina to continue."

S.FORM: Tinakoen ci Tafiti cowa ko fisac a wawa ni Yisi, kawra malo tato'asan ni Misiya cingra.
Tinakoen ci Tafiti cowa ko fisac a wawa ni Yisi, kawra malo tato'asan ni Misiya cingra.
S.PHON: tinako~uən ʦi tafiti ʦo~uwa ko~u fisaʦ a wawa ni jisi, kawra maɾo~u tato~uʡasan ni misija ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: David was not Jesse's firstborn son; yet, the Messiah descended from David.

S.FORM: Micadaay to ino' ci Rosi isaka 2009 a miheca, 2010 miheca mala misakafahay cingra.
Micadaay to ino' ci Rosi isaka 2009 a miheca, 2010 miheca mala misakafahay cingra.
S.PHON: miʦaɬ~ɮaaj to~u ino~uʡ ʦi ro~usi isaka **** a miħəʦa, **** miħəʦa maɾa misakafaħaj ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: Rose was baptized in 2009, and the next year, she began to serve as a regular pioneer.

S.FORM: Ci Ramalis tada mafana'ay micudad cingra.
Ci Ramalis tada mafana'ay micudad cingra.
S.PHON: ʦi ramaɾis taɬ~ɮa mafanaʡaj miʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: Damaris finished her basic schooling with top grades.

S.FORM: Samaanay ko demak ni Nowa, paihekal to pakaso'linay cingra to papafelien ni Yihofa ko saali ningra hani?
Samaanay ko demak ni Nowa, paihekal to pakaso'linay cingra to papafelien ni Yihofa ko saali ningra hani?
S.PHON: samaanaj ko~u ɬ~ɮəmak ni no~uwa, paiħəkaɾ to~u pakaso~uʡɾinaj ʦiŋra to~u papafəɾiən ni jiħo~ufa ko~u saaɾi niŋra ħani?
S.TRANSL: In what ways did Noah trust in Jehovah as his Provider?

S.FORM: Samaamanen no fafahi ni Potifar, mikayhid ci Yosif, hano caka masolot cingra hani?
Samaamanen no fafahi ni Potifar, mikayhid ci Yosif, hano caka masolot cingra hani?
S.PHON: samaamanən no~u fafaħi ni po~utifar, mikajħiɬ~ɮ ʦi jo~usif, ħano~u ʦaka maso~uɾo~ut ʦiŋra ħani?
S.TRANSL: What prevented Joseph from succumbing to her repeated advances?

S.FORM: Sa'ayaw somowalay ci Yihofa to adayayay ato sini'adaay Cingra, ao cowa pasowal to citanengay ato ci'icelay Cingra.
Sa'ayaw somowalay ci Yihofa to adayayay ato sini'adaay Cingra, ao cowa pasowal to citanengay ato ci'icelay Cingra.
S.PHON: saʡajaw so~umo~uwaɾaj ʦi jiħo~ufa to~u aɬ~ɮajajaj ato~u siniʡaɬ~ɮaaj ʦiŋra, ao~u ʦo~uwa paso~uwaɾ to~u ʦitanəŋaj ato~u ʦiʡiʦəɾaj ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: The first ones that he listed were mercy and compassion.

S.FORM: So'lin hatira, paso'linay cingra toya pafeliay to 'orip a Kawas, miharatengay cingranan, wa nga'ay miliyaw pa'orip cingranan noikor.
So'lin hatira, paso'linay cingra toya pafeliay to 'orip a Kawas, miharatengay cingranan, wa nga'ay miliyaw pa'orip cingranan noikor.
S.PHON: so~uʡɾin ħatira, paso~uʡɾinaj ʦiŋra to~uja pafəɾiaj to~u ʡo~urip a kawas, miħaratəŋaj ʦiŋranan, wa ŋaʡaj miɾijaw paʡo~urip ʦiŋranan no~uiko~ur.
S.TRANSL: Still, he trusted that the One who created human life in the first place could and would remember him and resurrect him.

S.FORM: O no'adingoan a tamdaw ko fafahi ningra, saheto o matatodongay ko ca'of ningra, 'arawhan ci Lopote cowa kafana' paca'of, mangodoay cingra.
O no'adingoan a tamdaw ko fafahi ningra, saheto o matatodongay ko ca'of ningra, 'arawhan ci Lopote cowa kafana' paca'of, mangodoay cingra.
S.PHON: o~u no~uʡaɬ~ɮiŋo~uan a tamɬ~ɮaw ko~u fafaħi niŋra, saħəto~u o~u matato~uɬ~ɮo~uŋaj ko~u ʦaʡo~uf niŋra, ʡarawħan ʦi ɾo~upo~utə ʦo~uwa kafanaʡ paʦaʡo~uf, maŋo~uɬ~ɮo~uaj ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: His wife, a spiritually strong person, had no problem answering the questions, but Robert found himself constantly embarrassed, not knowing what to say.

S.FORM: Hongti ci Tafiti pakaso'linay to papadamaen no Kawas ci Solomon, kawra, "o kapahho cingra, awaayho ko nilifetan ningra."
Hongti ci Tafiti pakaso'linay to papadamaen no Kawas ci Solomon, kawra, "o kapahho cingra, awaayho ko nilifetan ningra."
S.PHON: ħo~uŋti ʦi tafiti pakaso~uʡɾinaj to~u papaɬ~ɮamaən no~u kawas ʦi so~uɾo~umo~un, kawra, "o~u kapaħħo~u ʦiŋra, awaajħo~u ko~u niɾifətan niŋra."
S.TRANSL: King David was confident of God's support, but Solomon was "young and inexperienced."

S.FORM: Sanay cingra: 'Halafinay kako mafana'to so'linay kimad, misakafahayho kako itiyaho.

S.TRANSL: 'I've known the truth for years,' he said, 'and I've even served as a pioneer.

S.FORM: Ano pakararaw ato pakangolaw ko tamdaw i, iraay ko mihadidiay a tamdaw wa mitokel cingra mala kanga'ay to kalala'ed to tao.

S.TRANSL: For this reason, when a patient person is wronged or provoked, he refuses to give up hope for improvement in the disturbed relationship.

S.FORM: Mihaenay cowa pisatakaraw cingra, ciharatengay to tao, ato damsayay a padama to manikaway a tamdaw, mahaen ko sowal ningra: "Oya misanga'ay takowanan a Kawas i, caay ko misanga'ay cingraan saw?

S.PHON: miħaənaj ʦo~uwa pisatakaraw ʦiŋra, ʦiħaratəŋaj to~u tao~u, ato~u ɬ~ɮamsajaj a paɬ~ɮama to~u manikawaj a tamɬ~ɮaw, maħaən ko~u so~uwaɾ niŋra: "o~uja misaŋaʡaj tako~uwanan a kawas i, ʦaaj ko~u misaŋaʡaj ʦiŋraan saw?

S.FORM: Mikimad cingra i, sarakarakat sanay mikawing to kamay a mikimad, masieroday ko pikimad ningra, mihacecay a mitadtad to sinael.

Palalinian ni Pitiro ko micadaay to ino' ato misanga'an ni Nowa a tamina, sanay cingra: "Onini a nanom i, o todongto no pa'oripay tamowanan a ino'."

S.FORM: Mifana'ayho kitaanan, milimasmasay Cingra to sapatefoc, kinafalah to wawa no Niyah ci Yisan, paihkalay konini tada maolahay Cingra kitaanan.

S.FORM: O Kimad no Kawas ato fangcalay saocor ko padamaay ci Ripiroan, saikor mahelongto ko katelangay a pinangan, halo micada to ino', nohatini o malohemayto malitengay no kasakopang cingra.
O Kimad no Kawas ato fangcalay saocor ko padamaay ci Ripiroan, saikor mahelongto ko katelangay a pinangan, halo micada to ino', nohatini o malohemayto malitengay no kasakopang cingra.

Cowa ka ta'angay ko kapifiyor wa saan kita alatek, kawra nolatekay pairaay to ta'angayay a pasangar."

Kawra cowa ka nga'ay a miharateng kita to edengan oya makapahay ko loma' a salikaka ko papafeli to pilafinan no salikaka, alatek adihayayto ko micadaan nangra palafin.

Mihaenay, wa mikitini kita i cowa ko kalimlaan a demak 'mi'aca' to toki.

S.TRANSL: Granted, at times it is challenging, but life as a need-greater is very rewarding.

Cowa ka talaw kami,

S.TRANSL: But instead of fearing men,

S.TRANSL: He might reach out to unbelieving mates, help the inactive, conduct Bible studies, and expand his ministry.

S.FORM: So'lin cowa ka fana' kita a masasowal, wa nga'ay a pakalalok to tamdaw.
So'lin cowa ka fana' kita a masasowal, wa nga'ay a pakalalok to tamdaw.

S.TRANSL: It would be an error on our part if we believed that we cannot be encouraging because we are not particularly communicative.

S.FORM: Tahirato i masa'osiay a mihecaan nga' nga'ay micada to ino', cowa ka saan.
Tahirato i masa'osiay a mihecaan nga' nga'ay micada to ino', cowa ka saan.

S.FORM: Cowato pidofdof ko 'alomanay tamdaw to palinian no waywayan, cowa ka faheka' kita tonini.

S.TRANSL: It comes as no surprise, therefore, that moral standards are being abandoned.

S.FORM: Pakayni i "Lakoing", cowa ka hecahecad ko sowal no tamdaw.

Mahecaday, cowa ka hahecahecad ko kananam no tamdaw i faelohay kasakopang.

S.TRANSL: Likewise, not everyone adapts to a new congregation at the same pace.

S.FORM: O cowa ka sadafdaf a demak, milingatay to saka sasongtol no mirikecay.
O cowa ka sadafdaf a demak, milingatay to saka sasongtol no mirikecay.

S.TRANSL: Injustice often inflames political passions.

S.FORM: Tona cowa ka nga'ay ko kicay, manikaway ko 'orip no tamdaw.
Tona cowa ka nga'ay ko kicay, manikaway ko 'orip no tamdaw.

S.TRANSL: Life there was simple because it was a time of economic depression.

S.FORM: Teloc i, oniya padamaay sikawdan pakayni i noka kaemang hato cowa ka nilnilen.
Teloc i, oniya padamaay sikawdan pakayni i noka kaemang hato cowa ka nilnilen.

S.TRANSL: Sadly, the brother was held back from appointment simply because of the way he looked.

S.FORM: Harateng no roma tamdaw, o hatiniay a kero, cowa ko so'linay madadi'i to tamdaw, coko mararaway a waywayan sanay.
Harateng no roma tamdaw, o hatiniay a kero, cowa ko so'linay madadi'i to tamdaw, coko mararaway a waywayan sanay.
S.PHON: ħaratəŋ no~u ro~uma tamɬ~ɮaw, o~u ħatiniaj a kəro~u, ʦo~uwa ko~u so~uʡɾinaj maɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮiʡi to~u tamɬ~ɮaw, ʦo~uko~u mararawaj a wajwajan sanaj.
S.TRANSL: Some might excuse such conduct, reasoning that it is not the same as outright sexual relations.

S.FORM: O kahacecay a tamdaw cowa ka hecad ko toki a maadah ko somad no faloco'.
O kahacecay a tamdaw cowa ka hecad ko toki a maadah ko somad no faloco'.
S.PHON: o~u kaħaʦəʦaj a tamɬ~ɮaw ʦo~uwa ka ħəʦaɬ~ɮ ko~u to~uki a maaɬ~ɮaħ ko~u so~umaɬ~ɮ no~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ.
S.TRANSL: The grieving process can vary greatly from person to person.

S.FORM: O kowangkaw no misasingdiay misaka dademakay to haiyo a pacakay to dafong, mifekaday ko kalim no tamdaw sapi'acaaw tora dafong, 'arawhan cowa ko kalimelaan.
O kowangkaw no misasingdiay misaka dademakay to haiyo a pacakay to dafong, mifekaday ko kalim no tamdaw sapi'acaaw tora dafong, 'arawhan cowa ko kalimelaan.
S.PHON: o~u ko~uwaŋkaw no~u misasiŋɬ~ɮiaj misaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmakaj to~u ħaijo~u a paʦakaj to~u ɬ~ɮafo~uŋ, mifəkaɬ~ɮaj ko~u kaɾim no~u tamɬ~ɮaw sapiʡaʦaaw to~ura ɬ~ɮafo~uŋ, ʡarawħan ʦo~uwa ko~u kaɾiməɾaan.
S.TRANSL: Clever advertisers often feature attractive people deceptively promoting as must-haves many things we really do not need.

S.FORM: Somowal to tatiihay sanaysanay a sowal, cowa ko mamisalof to demak.
Somowal to tatiihay sanaysanay a sowal, cowa ko mamisalof to demak.
S.PHON: so~umo~uwaɾ to~u tatiiħaj sanajsanaj a so~uwaɾ, ʦo~uwa ko~u mamisaɾo~uf to~u ɬ~ɮəmak.
S.TRANSL: Whether we are right or wrong, engaging in hurtful speech will never improve matters.

S.FORM: Ira ko hatiniay harateng ita, cowa ko cango'otay ko pakaso'lin no faloco', pakayniay pisamacay no faloco' ita to mitosilay a demak.
Ira ko hatiniay harateng ita, cowa ko cango'otay ko pakaso'lin no faloco', pakayniay pisamacay no faloco' ita to mitosilay a demak.
S.PHON: ira ko~u ħatiniaj ħaratəŋ ita, ʦo~uwa ko~u ʦaŋo~uʡo~utaj ko~u pakaso~uʡɾin no~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ, pakajniaj pisamaʦaj no~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ ita to~u mito~usiɾaj a ɬ~ɮəmak.
S.TRANSL: Such feelings indicate, not that we lack faith, but that our heart is in the preaching work.

S.FORM: Misamaanay hano cisasa ko Israil tamdaw to demak ni Mosi i ikoray a Miripa, o cowa ko kafahka'an koni?
Misamaanay hano cisasa ko Israil tamdaw to demak ni Mosi i ikoray a Miripa, o cowa ko kafahka'an koni?
S.PHON: misamaanaj ħano~u ʦisasa ko~u israiɾ tamɬ~ɮaw to~u ɬ~ɮəmak ni mo~usi i iko~uraj a miripa, o~u ʦo~uwa ko~u kafaħkaʡan ko~uni?
S.TRANSL: How might Moses' action of striking the rock have detracted from the miracle Jehovah performed?

S.FORM: 'Alomanay ko tatosilen a tamdaw, cowa ko madoloay a demak.
'Alomanay ko tatosilen a tamdaw, cowa ko madoloay a demak.
S.PHON: ʡaɾo~umanaj ko~u tato~usiɾən a tamɬ~ɮaw, ʦo~uwa ko~u maɬ~ɮo~uɾo~uaj a ɬ~ɮəmak.
S.TRANSL: As you can imagine, the publishers have been able to contact only a small number of the inhabitants of this country.

S.FORM: CI LOPOTE 15 ko mihecaan micada to ino', kawra cowa pisakareteng to so'linay kimad.
CI LOPOTE 15 ko mihecaan micada to ino', kawra cowa pisakareteng to so'linay kimad.
S.PHON: ʦi ɾo~upo~utə ** ko~u miħəʦaan miʦaɬ~ɮa to~u ino~uʡ, kawra ʦo~uwa pisakarətəŋ to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: ROBERT got baptized as a teenager, but he did not really take the truth seriously.

S.FORM: Cowa, caay ka haen ko harateng ni Yopu.
Cowa, caay ka haen ko harateng ni Yopu.
S.PHON: ʦo~uwa, ʦaaj ka ħaən ko~u ħaratəŋ ni jo~upo~u~u.
S.TRANSL: God was not, and Job did not think so.

S.FORM: Calemcemay ci Mariya, samanen cowa ka fana'cingra, hano pasowal ko coyoh cingranan.
Calemcemay ci Mariya, samanen cowa ka fana'cingra, hano pasowal ko coyoh cingranan.
S.PHON: ʦaɾəmʦəmaj ʦi marija, samanən ʦo~uwa ka fanaʡʦiŋra, ħano~u paso~uwaɾ ko~u ʦo~ujo~uħ ʦiŋranan.
S.TRANSL: Mary was "deeply disturbed," uncertain why an angel was speaking to her.

S.FORM: Misatapang kita midoedo to so'linay kimad a ma'orip i, mafalicayto ko kalala'ed ita to cowa pihakelong a salawina.
Misatapang kita midoedo to so'linay kimad a ma'orip i, mafalicayto ko kalala'ed ita to cowa pihakelong a salawina.
S.PHON: misatapaŋ kita miɬ~ɮo~uəɬ~ɮo~u to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ a maʡo~urip i, mafaɾiʦajto~u ko~u kaɾaɾaʡəɬ~ɮ ita to~u ʦo~uwa piħakəɾo~uŋ a saɾawina.
S.TRANSL: When we started to walk in the truth, our relationship with non-Witness associates and family members changed.

S.FORM: Nolatekay, cowa pihakelong a raramod alatek papirienangay to wawa tono mararaway pihakelongan a lisin, anca pasifana' cangranan to mararaway a kimad.
Nolatekay, cowa pihakelong a raramod alatek papirienangay to wawa tono mararaway pihakelongan a lisin, anca pasifana' cangranan to mararaway a kimad.
S.PHON: no~uɾatəkaj, ʦo~uwa piħakəɾo~uŋ a raramo~uɬ~ɮ aɾatək papiriənaŋaj to~u wawa to~uno~u mararawaj piħakəɾo~uŋan a ɾisin, anʦa pasifanaʡ ʦaŋranan to~u mararawaj a kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: At times, unbelieving mates will demand that children share in pagan celebrations or be taught false religious beliefs.

S.FORM: So'lin ma'odangay ko ramod i, misamaanay o makoracay a tamdaw mipili' to cowa ko malalikong hani?
So'lin ma'odangay ko ramod i, misamaanay o makoracay a tamdaw mipili' to cowa ko malalikong hani?
S.PHON: so~uʡɾin maʡo~uɬ~ɮaŋaj ko~u ramo~uɬ~ɮ i, misamaanaj o~u mako~uraʦaj a tamɬ~ɮaw mipiɾiʡ to~u ʦo~uwa ko~u maɾaɾiko~uŋ ħani?
S.TRANSL: Why might a Christian decide not to get a divorce even though having Scriptural grounds to do so?

S.FORM: O roma alatek cowa ko papakaremo kako milaheci to tayal saan a miharateng.
O roma alatek cowa ko papakaremo kako milaheci to tayal saan a miharateng.
S.PHON: o~u ro~uma aɾatək ʦo~uwa ko~u papakarəmo~u kako~u miɾaħəʦi to~u tajaɾ saan a miħaratəŋ.
S.TRANSL: Another brother might have feelings of inadequacy about fulfilling an assignment.

S.FORM: Cowa ka fana' ci Yopu, wa hacowa mifekad ko miliyaway ma'orip noikor.
Cowa ka fana' ci Yopu, wa hacowa mifekad ko miliyaway ma'orip noikor.
S.PHON: ʦo~uwa ka fanaʡ ʦi jo~upo~u~u, wa ħaʦo~uwa mifəkaɬ~ɮ ko~u miɾijawaj maʡo~urip no~uiko~ur.
S.TRANSL: Job could not know when in the future that time would be.

S.FORM: O misamangahay a olah, o masaka ngodoan a demak, cowa kalecad to kinafalahay a olah.
O misamangahay a olah, o masaka ngodoan a demak, cowa kalecad to kinafalahay a olah.
S.PHON: o~u misamaŋaħaj a o~uɾaħ, o~u masaka ŋo~uɬ~ɮo~uan a ɬ~ɮəmak, ʦo~uwa kaɾəʦaɬ~ɮ to~u kinafaɾaħaj a o~uɾaħ.
S.TRANSL: Hypocritical love is especially shameful because it is a counterfeit of the godly quality of self-sacrificing love.

S.FORM: Ikorto mafana'ay kako, cowa kanga'ay ko mata no salikaka ci Frans, nikawrira, salikaka ci Nolo marariday mi'osi to lalakaw a patengil cingranan.
Ikorto mafana'ay kako, cowa kanga'ay ko mata no salikaka ci Frans, nikawrira, salikaka ci Nolo marariday mi'osi to lalakaw a patengil cingranan.
S.PHON: iko~urto~u mafanaʡaj kako~u, ʦo~uwa kaŋaʡaj ko~u mata no~u saɾikaka ʦi frans, nikawrira, saɾikaka ʦi no~uɾo~u marariɬ~ɮaj miʡo~usi to~u ɾaɾakaw a patəŋiɾ ʦiŋranan.
S.TRANSL: I later found out that because of Brother Franz' failing eyesight, Brother Knorr had been spending considerable time reading such material to him.

S.FORM: Pakayni i lakoing, iraay ko maanan cowa ka hecad a sowal no tamdaw hani?
Pakayni i lakoing, iraay ko maanan cowa ka hecad a sowal no tamdaw hani?
S.PHON: pakajni i ɾako~uiŋ, iraaj ko~u maanan ʦo~uwa ka ħəʦaɬ~ɮ a so~uwaɾ no~u tamɬ~ɮaw ħani?
S.TRANSL: What are some differing ideas about paradise?

S.FORM: Kawra, cowa pidama kiso ci Satanan, micada to ino' malo Krisciyang, wa adihay ko malifokay iso a saka lipahak.
Kawra, cowa pidama kiso ci Satanan, micada to ino' malo Krisciyang, wa adihay ko malifokay iso a saka lipahak.
S.PHON: kawra, ʦo~uwa piɬ~ɮama kiso~u ʦi satanan, miʦaɬ~ɮa to~u ino~uʡ maɾo~u krisʦijaŋ, wa aɬ~ɮiħaj ko~u maɾifo~ukaj iso~u a saka ɾipaħak.
S.TRANSL: In contrast to supporting Satan, consider the blessings you have as a dedicated and baptized Christian.

S.FORM: Hamaan palaken ita ko toki, minengneng to cowa ka so'lin a sinpong anca cowaho ka hapinangen a tikami no tingnaw.
Hamaan palaken ita ko toki, minengneng to cowa ka so'lin a sinpong anca cowaho ka hapinangen a tikami no tingnaw.
S.PHON: ħamaan paɾakən ita ko~u to~uki, minəŋnəŋ to~u ʦo~uwa ka so~uʡɾin a sinpo~uŋ anʦa ʦo~uwaħo~u ka ħapinaŋən a tikami no~u tiŋnaw.
S.TRANSL: We should not waste our time viewing questionable Internet news sites or reading unsubstantiated reports circulated via e-mail.
