Sawaden ko pihakelongan iso, minokayto!"
S.PHON: sawaɬ~ɮən ko~u piħakəɾo~uŋan iso~u, mino~ukajto~u!"
S.TRANSL: Renounce your activities and go home!"

S.FORM: Hano salalicayen ko malohemay Krisciyang to nisahalakaan iso hani?
Hano salalicayen ko malohemay Krisciyang to nisahalakaan iso hani?
S.PHON: ħano~u saɾaɾiʦajən ko~u maɾo~uħəmaj krisʦijaŋ to~u nisaħaɾakaan iso~u ħani?
S.TRANSL: Why is it worth discussing your plans for the future with experienced Christians?

S.FORM: Oniya ma'oningay ato paihekalan no madimadiay sikawdan a paso'linay harateng, wa samaan milingat to pinengneng iso to miliyaw ma'orip a hatokel?
Oniya ma'oningay ato paihekalan no madimadiay sikawdan a paso'linay harateng, wa samaan milingat to pinengneng iso to miliyaw ma'orip a hatokel?
S.PHON: o~unija maʡo~uniŋaj ato~u paiħəkaɾan no~u maɬ~ɮimaɬ~ɮiaj sikawɬ~ɮan a paso~uʡɾinaj ħaratəŋ, wa samaan miɾiŋat to~u pinəŋnəŋ iso~u to~u miɾijaw maʡo~urip a ħato~ukəɾ?
S.TRANSL: How should those events and the convictions held by other faithful ones of the past affect your hope?

S.FORM: O manan a nisahalakaan iso papalipahak to hanoikoran iso?
O manan a nisahalakaan iso papalipahak to hanoikoran iso?
S.PHON: o~u manan a nisaħaɾakaan iso~u papaɾipaħak to~u ħano~uiko~uran iso~u?
S.TRANSL: What plans can lead to a happy future for you?

S.FORM: O wama wina, ano pasangaray ko wawa iso mananam i faelohay kasakopang, palaken ko toki malacafay to wawa a mitolon.
O wama wina, ano pasangaray ko wawa iso mananam i faelohay kasakopang, palaken ko toki malacafay to wawa a mitolon.
S.PHON: o~u wama wina, ano~u pasaŋaraj ko~u wawa iso~u mananam i faəɾo~uħaj kasako~upaŋ, paɾakən ko~u to~uki maɾaʦafaj to~u wawa a mito~uɾo~un.
S.TRANSL: Parents, if your children are struggling to fit in, take time to pray with them about this matter.

S.FORM: So'lin awaay ko milifetan iso a patireng, wa nga'ay pasadak to 'icel.
So'lin awaay ko milifetan iso a patireng, wa nga'ay pasadak to 'icel.
S.PHON: so~uʡɾin awaaj ko~u miɾifətan iso~u a patirəŋ, wa ŋaʡaj pasaɬ~ɮak to~u ʡiʦəɾ.
S.TRANSL: Whether you are skilled or not, can you make yourself available?

S.FORM: Samaanen iso pa'icel ato mihinom ko mararomay ko faloco' a tamdaw hani?
Samaanen iso pa'icel ato mihinom ko mararomay ko faloco' a tamdaw hani?
S.PHON: samaanən iso~u paʡiʦəɾ ato~u miħino~um ko~u mararo~umaj ko~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ a tamɬ~ɮaw ħani?
S.TRANSL: How can you strengthen and console those who have "a crushed spirit"?

S.FORM: Itini cowa ka olah to so'linay kimad a kilac mitosil, ano matoled ko faloco' iso, wa nga'ay haratengen ko milifetan ni Pawlo.
Itini cowa ka olah to so'linay kimad a kilac mitosil, ano matoled ko faloco' iso, wa nga'ay haratengen ko milifetan ni Pawlo.
S.PHON: itini ʦo~uwa ka o~uɾaħ to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ a kiɾaʦ mito~usiɾ, ano~u mato~uɾəɬ~ɮ ko~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ iso~u, wa ŋaʡaj ħaratəŋən ko~u miɾifətan ni pawɾo~u.
S.TRANSL: If you have ever felt discouraged when preaching in less responsive territories, you will relate to the apostle Paul.

S.FORM: Samaan ko ca'of iso?
Samaan ko ca'of iso?
S.PHON: samaan ko~u ʦaʡo~uf iso~u?
S.TRANSL: Would you say...

S.FORM: Alatek awaay ko hatiniay a nalifetan iso, kawra wa nga'ayho kiso nai mi'oningan i saka 2 Samuil a mafana' to harateng no mato'asay a salikaka.
Alatek awaay ko hatiniay a nalifetan iso, kawra wa nga'ayho kiso nai mi'oningan i saka 2 Samuil a mafana' to harateng no mato'asay a salikaka.
S.PHON: aɾatək awaaj ko~u ħatiniaj a naɾifətan iso~u, kawra wa ŋaʡajħo~u kiso~u nai miʡo~uniŋan i saka * samo~u~uiɾ a mafanaʡ to~u ħaratəŋ no~u mato~uʡasaj a saɾikaka.
S.TRANSL: Even if you have not personally experienced feelings like those mentioned earlier, an account in 2 Samuel may help you to understand those who have.

S.FORM: Nga'ayay 'osien iso itini i jw.org. wangcan a lalakaw, "Fangcalay Cudad Mifalicay to Ka'orip no Tamdaw," sanay a lalakaw, minengneng to nalifetan nangra.
Nga'ayay 'osien iso itini i jw.org. wangcan a lalakaw, "Fangcalay Cudad Mifalicay to Ka'orip no Tamdaw," sanay a lalakaw, minengneng to nalifetan nangra.
S.PHON: ŋaʡajaj ʡo~usiən iso~u itini i jw.o~ur*. waŋʦan a ɾaɾakaw, "faŋʦaɾaj ʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ mifaɾiʦaj to~u kaʡo~urip no~u tamɬ~ɮaw," sanaj a ɾaɾakaw, minəŋnəŋ to~u naɾifətan naŋra.
S.TRANSL: You can read some of their experiences in the series "The Bible Changes Lives," found on jw.org.

S.FORM: Ano miliyasay ci Yihofaan ko paro no loma' iso i, o manan ko papadama tisowanan pakaremo to nika adada no faloco'?
Ano miliyasay ci Yihofaan ko paro no loma' iso i, o manan ko papadama tisowanan pakaremo to nika adada no faloco'?
S.PHON: ano~u miɾijasaj ʦi jiħo~ufaan ko~u paro~u no~u ɾo~umaʡ iso~u i, o~u manan ko~u papaɬ~ɮama tiso~uwanan pakarəmo~u to~u nika aɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮa no~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ?
S.TRANSL: What can help you to cope if a family member leaves Jehovah?

S.FORM: Samaanen iso miremo ko talaw, mikifaloco' a romadiw pahemek ci Yihofaan?
Samaanen iso miremo ko talaw, mikifaloco' a romadiw pahemek ci Yihofaan?
S.PHON: samaanən iso~u mirəmo~u ko~u taɾaw, mikifaɾo~uʦo~uʡ a ro~umaɬ~ɮiw paħəmək ʦi jiħo~ufaan?
S.TRANSL: How can you overcome obstacles and sing praises to Jehovah with feeling?

S.FORM: Sasamaanen iso padama ko malinahay tayni kitakit no miso a tamdaw hani?
Sasamaanen iso padama ko malinahay tayni kitakit no miso a tamdaw hani?
S.PHON: sasamaanən iso~u paɬ~ɮama ko~u maɾinaħaj tajni kitakit no~u miso~u a tamɬ~ɮaw ħani?
S.TRANSL: What help can you offer to newcomers in your area?

S.FORM: Misamaanan misalof koya faelohay fangcalay radiw, samaanen iso kalasaka dademak koni faelohay fangcalay radiw hani?
Misamaanan misalof koya faelohay fangcalay radiw, samaanen iso kalasaka dademak koni faelohay fangcalay radiw hani?
S.PHON: misamaanan misaɾo~uf ko~uja faəɾo~uħaj faŋʦaɾaj raɬ~ɮiw, samaanən iso~u kaɾasaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmak ko~uni faəɾo~uħaj faŋʦaɾaj raɬ~ɮiw ħani?
S.TRANSL: What improvements have been made in the new songbook, and how can you make the best use of it?

S.FORM: O tokel ni Yihofa tisowanan, misahalaka to hanoikoran i, o noka olah to Kawas ato tamdaw ko patosokan noka'orip iso.
O tokel ni Yihofa tisowanan, misahalaka to hanoikoran i, o noka olah to Kawas ato tamdaw ko patosokan noka'orip iso.
S.PHON: o~u to~ukəɾ ni jiħo~ufa tiso~uwanan, misaħaɾaka to~u ħano~uiko~uran i, o~u no~uka o~uɾaħ to~u kawas ato~u tamɬ~ɮaw ko~u pato~uso~ukan no~ukaʡo~urip iso~u.
S.TRANSL: Therefore, Jehovah desires that your plans focus on demonstrating love for others and for God.

S.FORM: Sapilifetaw to kanga'ay ni Yihofa a roma pakayraan i, pafelito timolo tisowanan mifana' to tamdaw to pidoyongan iso.
Sapilifetaw to kanga'ay ni Yihofa a roma pakayraan i, pafelito timolo tisowanan mifana' to tamdaw to pidoyongan iso.
S.PHON: sapiɾifətaw to~u kaŋaʡaj ni jiħo~ufa a ro~uma pakajraan i, pafəɾito~u timo~uɾo~u tiso~uwanan mifanaʡ to~u tamɬ~ɮaw to~u piɬ~ɮo~ujo~uŋan iso~u.
S.TRANSL: Another way you can taste Jehovah's goodness is by experiencing his support as you share your faith with others.

S.FORM: olah Iso.
olah Iso.
S.PHON: o~uɾaħ iso~u.
S.TRANSL: that could ever be shown.

S.FORM: Ano pakayniay nipihakelong iso ci Yisan a macoli no paro no loma', samaanen iso ko demak?
Ano pakayniay nipihakelong iso ci Yisan a macoli no paro no loma', samaanen iso ko demak?
S.PHON: ano~u pakajniaj nipiħakəɾo~uŋ iso~u ʦi jisan a maʦo~uɾi no~u paro~u no~u ɾo~umaʡ, samaanən iso~u ko~u ɬ~ɮəmak?
S.TRANSL: What should you do if you experience family division because you are following Jesus?

S.FORM: Taneng 3:9 sanay: "Alaen ko safangcalay a kinaira no omah iso a pacakat to sapahemek ci Yihofaan."
Taneng 3:9 sanay: "Alaen ko safangcalay a kinaira no omah iso a pacakat to sapahemek ci Yihofaan."
S.PHON: tanəŋ *:* sanaj: "aɾaən ko~u safaŋʦaɾaj a kinaira no~u o~umaħ iso~u a paʦakat to~u sapaħəmək ʦi jiħo~ufaan."
S.TRANSL: Proverbs 3:9 says: "Honor Jehovah with your valuable things."

S.FORM: Itini satolon wa nga'ay hadaken iso ko ngangan no nima hani?
Itini satolon wa nga'ay hadaken iso ko ngangan no nima hani?
S.PHON: itini sato~uɾo~un wa ŋaʡaj ħaɬ~ɮakən iso~u ko~u ŋaŋan no~u nima ħani?
S.TRANSL: Think of those whom you can pray for by name.

S.FORM: Alatek o saali no tireng ko saali iso.
Alatek o saali no tireng ko saali iso.
S.PHON: aɾatək o~u saaɾi no~u tirəŋ ko~u saaɾi iso~u.
S.TRANSL: Sometimes your needs may be physical.

S.FORM: O nano tayal iso isalakat nga'ay padama to 'alomanay, tangongolay ko pifiyor cangranan, nohatini kamayan nga'ay kiso padotoc a padama to tamdaw.
O nano tayal iso isalakat nga'ay padama to 'alomanay, tangongolay ko pifiyor cangranan, nohatini kamayan nga'ay kiso padotoc a padama to tamdaw.
S.PHON: o~u nano~u tajaɾ iso~u isaɾakat ŋaʡaj paɬ~ɮama to~u ʡaɾo~umanaj, taŋo~uŋo~uɾaj ko~u pifijo~ur ʦaŋranan, no~uħatini kamajan ŋaʡaj kiso~u paɬ~ɮo~uto~uʦ a paɬ~ɮama to~u tamɬ~ɮaw.
S.TRANSL: You have already touched the lives of countless people with your heartfelt work, and you can continue to do so.

S.FORM: Mikitini i 'Olic saka 3 fedengan wa nga'ay mahela ita ko sapaca'of.
Mikitini i 'Olic saka 3 fedengan wa nga'ay mahela ita ko sapaca'of.
S.PHON: mikitini i ʡo~uɾiʦ saka * fəɬ~ɮəŋan wa ŋaʡaj maħəɾa ita ko~u sapaʦaʡo~uf.
S.TRANSL: We gain insight into this by examining the third Psalm.

S.FORM: O manan ko mamananam ita nai 'Olic 147 fedengan saka 4 sinael?
O manan ko mamananam ita nai 'Olic 147 fedengan saka 4 sinael?
S.PHON: o~u manan ko~u mamananam ita nai ʡo~uɾiʦ *** fəɬ~ɮəŋan saka * sinaəɾ?
S.TRANSL: How can we benefit from the psalmist's seeming change of subject at Psalm 147:4?

S.FORM: Samacayen ita miharateng ko iraayto a sapalemed no niyah, halo mihadidi a mitala to ka'ayaway a hatokel.
Samacayen ita miharateng ko iraayto a sapalemed no niyah, halo mihadidi a mitala to ka'ayaway a hatokel.
S.PHON: samaʦajən ita miħaratəŋ ko~u iraajto~u a sapaɾəməɬ~ɮ no~u nijaħ, ħaɾo~u miħaɬ~ɮiɬ~ɮi a mitaɾa to~u kaʡajawaj a ħato~ukəɾ.
S.TRANSL: Rather than lose our joy, we can focus on the good things we have now and wait patiently for the better things ahead.

S.FORM: Losiyangen ita i polong no hekal.
Losiyangen ita i polong no hekal.
S.PHON: ɾo~usijaŋən ita i po~uɾo~uŋ no~u ħəkaɾ.
S.TRANSL: And declare it to ev'ry tribe and nation.

S.FORM: Kawra macakatto ko no'adingoan ita i, o papasowalento ita to nika pihakelong no mita.
Kawra macakatto ko no'adingoan ita i, o papasowalento ita to nika pihakelong no mita.
S.PHON: kawra maʦakatto~u ko~u no~uʡaɬ~ɮiŋo~uan ita i, o~u papaso~uwaɾənto~u ita to~u nika piħakəɾo~uŋ no~u mita.
S.TRANSL: As our faith grew, though, we saw the need to be open about our beliefs.

S.FORM: O tatokelen ita mitala ko manan?
O tatokelen ita mitala ko manan?
S.PHON: o~u tato~ukəɾən ita mitaɾa ko~u manan?
S.TRANSL: What hope can we maintain?

S.FORM: Mikitini i nalifetan ni Solomon, mananamay ita ko maanan?
Mikitini i nalifetan ni Solomon, mananamay ita ko maanan?
S.PHON: mikitini i naɾifətan ni so~uɾo~umo~un, mananamaj ita ko~u maanan?
S.TRANSL: What can you learn from Solomon's experiment with pleasures?

S.FORM: Kahacecay ita iraay ko mikiriwi'ay a pinangan, mankora ciwidangay kita to masasi romaromaay a tamdaw.
Kahacecay ita iraay ko mikiriwi'ay a pinangan, mankora ciwidangay kita to masasi romaromaay a tamdaw.
S.PHON: kaħaʦəʦaj ita iraaj ko~u mikiriwiʡaj a pinaŋan, manko~ura ʦiwiɬ~ɮaŋaj kita to~u masasi ro~umaro~umaaj a tamɬ~ɮaw.
S.TRANSL: Personality, the unique set of traits that each of us has and that varies from one person to another, can make for rich friendships.

S.FORM: Mikikakaay kitanan ko nika fana' to manan ko saali ita.
Mikikakaay kitanan ko nika fana' to manan ko saali ita.
S.PHON: mikikakaaj kitanan ko~u nika fanaʡ to~u manan ko~u saaɾi ita.
S.TRANSL: He cares for us better than we can care for ourselves.

S.FORM: Tano sowalen ita,
Tano sowalen ita,
S.PHON: tano~u so~uwaɾən ita,
S.TRANSL: By our reassuring words,

S.FORM: Ti'eren ita ko sadama nangra, wa nga'ay miconged to 'ada.
Ti'eren ita ko sadama nangra, wa nga'ay miconged to 'ada.
S.PHON: tiʡərən ita ko~u saɬ~ɮama naŋra, wa ŋaʡaj miʦo~uŋəɬ~ɮ to~u ʡaɬ~ɮa.
S.TRANSL: With their help, we can resist our enemy.

S.FORM: Kalasaka dademaken ita ko maanan toki patahekal to mihamhamay?
Kalasaka dademaken ita ko maanan toki patahekal to mihamhamay?
S.PHON: kaɾasaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmakən ita ko~u maanan to~uki pataħəkaɾ to~u miħamħamaj?
S.TRANSL: opportunities we have to extend hospitality?

S.FORM: Kawra palalinien to malifokay a sapalemed, o misadakan ita a sapi'aca, cowato ka samaan.
Kawra palalinien to malifokay a sapalemed, o misadakan ita a sapi'aca, cowato ka samaan.
S.PHON: kawra paɾaɾiniən to~u maɾifo~ukaj a sapaɾəməɬ~ɮ, o~u misaɬ~ɮakan ita a sapiʡaʦa, ʦo~uwato~u ka samaan.
S.TRANSL: However, the price we paid is small in comparison to the blessings that we have received.

S.FORM: Mikitini milifetan ni Lopote mananamay ita ko maanan?
Mikitini milifetan ni Lopote mananamay ita ko maanan?
S.PHON: mikitini miɾifətan ni ɾo~upo~utə mananamaj ita ko~u maanan?
S.TRANSL: What can we learn from Robert's experience?

S.FORM: Mikitini i pipasifana'ni Pawlo to misimaway to kapalitan, mananamay ita ko maanan?
Mikitini i pipasifana'ni Pawlo to misimaway to kapalitan, mananamay ita ko maanan?
S.PHON: mikitini i pipasifanaʡni pawɾo~u to~u misimawaj to~u kapaɾitan, mananamaj ita ko~u maanan?
S.TRANSL: What lessons can we learn from the account about Paul and the jailer?

S.FORM: O roma, wa miharateng kita to sasamaan pakalalok to cafay ita a mitosil.
O roma, wa miharateng kita to sasamaan pakalalok to cafay ita a mitosil.
S.PHON: o~u ro~uma, wa miħaratəŋ kita to~u sasamaan pakaɾaɾo~uk to~u ʦafaj ita a mito~usiɾ.
S.TRANSL: We should also consider how to encourage our fellow workers.

S.FORM: Wa tano masamanay pakayraan ko kasasowal ita to tamdaw?
Wa tano masamanay pakayraan ko kasasowal ita to tamdaw?
S.PHON: wa tano~u masamanaj pakajraan ko~u kasaso~uwaɾ ita to~u tamɬ~ɮaw?
S.TRANSL: How should we speak to people?

S.FORM: Mikitini i sarikec ni Pawlo, wa mananam ita ko manan?
Mikitini i sarikec ni Pawlo, wa mananam ita ko manan?
S.PHON: mikitini i sarikəʦ ni pawɾo~u, wa mananam ita ko~u manan?
S.TRANSL: What do Paul's words about the golden calf episode teach us?

S.FORM: Samaanen ita malo so'li'linay a tamdaw?
Samaanen ita malo so'li'linay a tamdaw?
S.PHON: samaanən ita maɾo~u so~uʡɾiʡɾinaj a tamɬ~ɮaw?
S.TRANSL: How can we show that we are truthful with one another?

S.FORM: O maanan ko mananamay ita to pikerid ni Kristo to saka 1 hahekalan a kasakopang?
O maanan ko mananamay ita to pikerid ni Kristo to saka 1 hahekalan a kasakopang?
S.PHON: o~u maanan ko~u mananamaj ita to~u pikəriɬ~ɮ ni kristo~u to~u saka * ħaħəkaɾan a kasako~upaŋ?
S.TRANSL: What do we learn from the way Christ led the first-century congregation?

S.FORM: O roma a lalangian ita a mitosil o maanan hani?
O roma a lalangian ita a mitosil o maanan hani?
S.PHON: o~u ro~uma a ɾaɾaŋian ita a mito~usiɾ o~u maanan ħani?
S.TRANSL: What further reason for preaching do we have?

S.FORM: Tenaken ita.
Tenaken ita.
S.PHON: tənakən ita.
S.TRANSL: Boldly we proclaim.

S.FORM: Pakayni to raraw no tao, mikitini i nalifetan ni Mosi o mananam ita ko maanan?
Pakayni to raraw no tao, mikitini i nalifetan ni Mosi o mananam ita ko maanan?
S.PHON: pakajni to~u raraw no~u tao~u, mikitini i naɾifətan ni mo~usi o~u mananam ita ko~u maanan?
S.TRANSL: What lesson should we learn from Moses' reaction to the mistakes of others?

S.FORM: "Wamaaw, nanay, matiya o nika lacecay ita ko nika lacecay nangra."
"Wamaaw, nanay, matiya o nika lacecay ita ko nika lacecay nangra."
S.PHON: "wamaaw, nanaj, matija o~u nika ɾaʦəʦaj ita ko~u nika ɾaʦəʦaj naŋra."
S.TRANSL: "I make request... that they may all be one, just as you, Father, are in union with me."

S.FORM: Wa kilimen ita ko ca'of noniya salicay, wa milengoho to o maanan ko mamananam no mita.
Wa kilimen ita ko ca'of noniya salicay, wa milengoho to o maanan ko mamananam no mita.
S.PHON: wa kiɾimən ita ko~u ʦaʡo~uf no~unija saɾiʦaj, wa miɾəŋo~uħo~u to~u o~u maanan ko~u mamananam no~u mita.
S.TRANSL: As we discuss these questions, we will also consider the lessons we can learn.

S.FORM: O sasongila'en ita misaka dademak ko casi padama to tamdaw misinanot to kalimlaan a demak i noka'orip.
O sasongila'en ita misaka dademak ko casi padama to tamdaw misinanot to kalimlaan a demak i noka'orip.
S.PHON: o~u saso~uŋiɾaʡən ita misaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmak ko~u ʦasi paɬ~ɮama to~u tamɬ~ɮaw misinano~ut to~u kaɾimɾaan a ɬ~ɮəmak i no~ukaʡo~urip.
S.TRANSL: We should use them as tools to stimulate a person's interest in what really matters in life today.

S.FORM: Mikitini toniya ma'oningay, mananamay ita ko maanan hani?
Mikitini toniya ma'oningay, mananamay ita ko maanan hani?
S.PHON: mikitini to~unija maʡo~uniŋaj, mananamaj ita ko~u maanan ħani?
S.TRANSL: What can we learn from this account?

S.FORM: Mikitini tinakoan ni Solomon wa adihayay ko mananamay ita.
Mikitini tinakoan ni Solomon wa adihayay ko mananamay ita.
S.PHON: mikitini tinako~uan ni so~uɾo~umo~un wa aɬ~ɮiħajaj ko~u mananamaj ita.
S.TRANSL: We can learn much from the example of King Solomon.

S.FORM: Samaan ko picada ita to matoleday a salikaka hani?
Samaan ko picada ita to matoleday a salikaka hani?
S.PHON: samaan ko~u piʦaɬ~ɮa ita to~u mato~uɾəɬ~ɮaj a saɾikaka ħani?
S.TRANSL: What balanced view of our brothers will help us build them up in love?

S.FORM: O tatooren ita ko nisowalan.

S.TRANSL: Yes, like Paul, we can endure in bearing fruit.

S.TRANSL: But the courts did not agree, so I was sentenced to 31 days in prison.

S.FORM: Misamaan kako miremo to talaw, mahemeken a romadiw hani?
Misamaan kako miremo to talaw, mahemeken a romadiw hani?
S.PHON: misamaan kako~u mirəmo~u to~u taɾaw, maħəməkən a ro~umaɬ~ɮiw ħani?
S.TRANSL: How can I overcome any apprehension that may hold me back from singing out joyfully?

S.FORM: Ikorto miekoan koniya demak i hoing, kako ko mihadakay to saeko.
Ikorto miekoan koniya demak i hoing, kako ko mihadakay to saeko.
S.PHON: iko~urto~u miəko~uan ko~unija ɬ~ɮəmak i ħo~uiŋ, kako~u ko~u miħaɬ~ɮakaj to~u saəko~u.
S.TRANSL: The local priest threatened us with violence if we did not leave the village at once.

S.FORM: Sowal ningra: "Pakaso'linay kako iraay ko Kawas."
Sowal ningra: "Pakaso'linay kako iraay ko Kawas."
S.PHON: so~uwaɾ niŋra: "pakaso~uʡɾinaj kako~u iraaj ko~u kawas."
S.TRANSL: "There has to be a God," he said.

S.TRANSL: At the time, the local congregation seemed to me like an old-ladies' club.

S.FORM: "Mafoti'ay ko idang ita, tayra kako a mipalal i cingraan."

S.PHON: "mafo~utiʡaj ko~u iɬ~ɮaŋ ita, tajra kako~u a mipaɾaɾ i ʦiŋraan."

S.FORM: Ci Maeke, 19 miheca matayal to miliso'ay, sanay ko sowal: "O fa'inay no roma salikaka i kasakopang cowaho ko salikaka, wa nga'ayto kako a padama cangranan."
Ci Maeke, 19 miheca matayal to miliso'ay, sanay ko sowal: "O fa'inay no roma salikaka i kasakopang cowaho ko salikaka, wa nga'ayto kako a padama cangranan."

S.TRANSL: "My new circumstances allow me to reach out to the unbelieving husbands of sisters in our local congregation," says Marco, who was a traveling overseer for 19 years.

S.FORM: Kako ato mitepangay to cudad a kantok tayraay i ngata no fenpo, pakalomaloma'an a milicay to iraay ko makahiyay paliwal to sera hokira.
Kako ato mitepangay to cudad a kantok tayraay i ngata no fenpo, pakalomaloma'an a milicay to iraay ko makahiyay paliwal to sera hokira.

S.TRANSL: The printery overseer and I went from house to house in the neighborhood of the branch, asking whether anyone wanted to sell his property.

Alatek malitengayto kako!
S.PHON: aɾatək maɾitəŋajto~u kako~u!
S.TRANSL: I think that age has caught up with me.

S.FORM: O adihayay sasowalen cowaho ka tahatengil kako, kawra, mahemekay kako to adihayay ko casi parakatan tayra maraayay a pala.
O adihayay sasowalen cowaho ka tahatengil kako, kawra, mahemekay kako to adihayay ko casi parakatan tayra maraayay a pala.

S.TRANSL: I had never heard of many of the languages but was happy to know that tens of thousands of magazines were being sent to far-flung places.

Mansa mihaen ko sowal ningra: 'O makoracay kako, cowa citadah to palakisay to remes a raraw.'

S.TRANSL: I wanted to tell them about Jehovah, so after a while I made plans to learn their language.

Itira milakowitay a macokacok to tokotokosan, maraayay ko rarakaten, kawra, mahemekay kami tatosa padama to itiraay a salikaka.

S.TRANSL: However, their use became so common in the everyday life of Jesus' contemporaries that there was the tendency to reinforce practically any utterance by swearing to it.

S.FORM: Sowal no roma tamdaw madimadi to Kawas, kawra noka'orip nangra cowa ka ngodo to palinian no Kawas.
Sowal no roma tamdaw madimadi to Kawas, kawra noka'orip nangra cowa ka ngodo to palinian no Kawas.
S.PHON: so~uwaɾ no~u ro~uma tamɬ~ɮaw maɬ~ɮimaɬ~ɮi to~u kawas, kawra no~ukaʡo~urip naŋra ʦo~uwa ka ŋo~uɬ~ɮo~u to~u paɾinian no~u kawas.
S.TRANSL: Some individuals, though, pursue a course of disobedience while pretending to be faithful to God.

S.FORM: Kawra, calemcemay kiso halatek.
Kawra, calemcemay kiso halatek.
S.PHON: kawra, ʦaɾəmʦəmaj kiso~u ħaɾatək.
S.TRANSL: Understandably, though, you may also feel a little anxious.

S.FORM: Na mafokilay ci Taniayer tono Padaka sowal, 'ayaw no tayra Kiliyat micudad to lima folad, satapang sanayho minanam tono Padaka sowal, kawra ikorto malo idahiyay a mihongyakay.
Na mafokilay ci Taniayer tono Padaka sowal, 'ayaw no tayra Kiliyat micudad to lima folad, satapang sanayho minanam tono Padaka sowal, kawra ikorto malo idahiyay a mihongyakay.
S.PHON: na mafo~ukiɾaj ʦi taniajər to~uno~u paɬ~ɮaka so~uwaɾ, ʡajaw no~u tajra kiɾijat miʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ to~u ɾima fo~uɾaɬ~ɮ, satapaŋ sanajħo~u minanam to~uno~u paɬ~ɮaka so~uwaɾ, kawra iko~urto~u maɾo~u iɬ~ɮaħijaj a miħo~uŋjakaj.
S.TRANSL: Danièle, who had learned English within five months before attending Gilead, became a skilled translator of our publications.

S.FORM: Kawra, ano halafinayto milifok kiso to sasodod, kamayan a misemsem ko faloco', wa samaanto aki?
Kawra, ano halafinayto milifok kiso to sasodod, kamayan a misemsem ko faloco', wa samaanto aki?
S.PHON: kawra, ano~u ħaɾafinajto~u miɾifo~uk kiso~u to~u saso~uɬ~ɮo~uɬ~ɮ, kamajan a misəmsəm ko~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ, wa samaanto~u aki?
S.TRANSL: But suppose that long after a transgression was forgiven, you still have pangs of conscience over past mistakes?

S.FORM: Cowa ka pihaen ci Yihofa, kawra pasalatan Ningra papilifet ci Yopuan, mihaen a pasowal ci Satanan: "Miso sa a harateng a miala to dafong ningra a maemin."
Cowa ka pihaen ci Yihofa, kawra pasalatan Ningra papilifet ci Yopuan, mihaen a pasowal ci Satanan: "Miso sa a harateng a miala to dafong ningra a maemin."
S.PHON: ʦo~uwa ka piħaən ʦi jiħo~ufa, kawra pasaɾatan niŋra papiɾifət ʦi jo~upo~u~uan, miħaən a paso~uwaɾ ʦi satanan: "miso~u sa a ħaratəŋ a miaɾa to~u ɬ~ɮafo~uŋ niŋra a maəmin."
S.TRANSL: Jehovah did not do that, but he allowed Satan to test Job, stating: "Everything that he has is in your hand."

S.FORM: Kawra, kalikiay a tahasi'ayaw to saconged no 'ada.
Kawra, kalikiay a tahasi'ayaw to saconged no 'ada.
S.PHON: kawra, kaɾikiaj a taħasiʡajaw to~u saʦo~uŋəɬ~ɮ no~u ʡaɬ~ɮa.
S.TRANSL: Then the opposition came, hard and fast.

S.FORM: O sifana'ko kola no taneng, kawra o taneng milingatay to misa'osian no mita.
O sifana'ko kola no taneng, kawra o taneng milingatay to misa'osian no mita.
S.PHON: o~u sifanaʡko~u ko~uɾa no~u tanəŋ, kawra o~u tanəŋ miɾiŋataj to~u misaʡo~usian no~u mita.
S.TRANSL: Although wisdom is based on knowledge, it particularly has to do with the decisions we make rather than just the facts we understand.

S.FORM: Kawra, o sowal ni Aspen: "Mikilim to katayalan cowa ka haen o maharatengay niyam a madolo."
Kawra, o sowal ni Aspen: "Mikilim to katayalan cowa ka haen o maharatengay niyam a madolo."
S.PHON: kawra, o~u so~uwaɾ ni aspən: "mikiɾim to~u katajaɾan ʦo~uwa ka ħaən o~u maħaratəŋaj nijam a maɬ~ɮo~uɾo~u."
S.TRANSL: "Unfortunately," recounts Osborne, "secular work did not come as soon as we had hoped."

S.FORM: Kawra, saka dademaken ita ko picekiwan a toki midotim to harateng ni Yihofa, ano malifa ita ko maanan a pakayraan i, wa kaliki maharateng ko micekiwan a lalakaw, hanaca misaka dademak to kalimlaan.
Kawra, saka dademaken ita ko picekiwan a toki midotim to harateng ni Yihofa, ano malifa ita ko maanan a pakayraan i, wa kaliki maharateng ko micekiwan a lalakaw, hanaca misaka dademak to kalimlaan.
S.PHON: kawra, saka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmakən ita ko~u piʦəkiwan a to~uki miɬ~ɮo~utim to~u ħaratəŋ ni jiħo~ufa, ano~u maɾifa ita ko~u maanan a pakajraan i, wa kaɾiki maħaratəŋ ko~u miʦəkiwan a ɾaɾakaw, ħanaʦa misaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmak to~u kaɾimɾaan.
S.TRANSL: But if we meditate on Jehovah's thinking during personal study, we will be more likely to recall what we have studied and be able to apply it to the specific situation at hand.

S.FORM: Kawra mihadidiay ci Yihofa a mitengil to picangacang no madimadiay sikawdan Ningra.
Kawra mihadidiay ci Yihofa a mitengil to picangacang no madimadiay sikawdan Ningra.
S.PHON: kawra miħaɬ~ɮiɬ~ɮiaj ʦi jiħo~ufa a mitəŋiɾ to~u piʦaŋaʦaŋ no~u maɬ~ɮimaɬ~ɮiaj sikawɬ~ɮan niŋra.
S.TRANSL: Yet, Jehovah listens patiently to humans who call on him in faith.

S.FORM: O faelohay tayra a milakowitay to riyar a mitosilay, minanam to no Frans a sowal cowa ko madoloay, kawra makahiay mitengil to nga'ayay lihaf ko itiraay a tamdaw.
O faelohay tayra a milakowitay to riyar a mitosilay, minanam to no Frans a sowal cowa ko madoloay, kawra makahiay mitengil to nga'ayay lihaf ko itiraay a tamdaw.
S.PHON: o~u faəɾo~uħaj tajra a miɾako~uwitaj to~u rijar a mito~usiɾaj, minanam to~u no~u frans a so~uwaɾ ʦo~uwa ko~u maɬ~ɮo~uɾo~uaj, kawra makaħiaj mitəŋiɾ to~u ŋaʡajaj ɾiħaf ko~u itiraaj a tamɬ~ɮaw.
S.TRANSL: Most new missionaries there struggled to learn French, but people listened.

S.FORM: Itini i romaroma serangawan, ano fahal sa tayra miliso' to tamdaw, kamayanay a patahekal to mihamhamay to ciloma'ay; kawra o roma i, pa'ayawen pasowal nga' tayra miliso'.
Itini i romaroma serangawan, ano fahal sa tayra miliso' to tamdaw, kamayanay a patahekal to mihamhamay to ciloma'ay; kawra o roma i, pa'ayawen pasowal nga' tayra miliso'.
S.PHON: itini i ro~umaro~uma səraŋawan, ano~u faħaɾ sa tajra miɾiso~uʡ to~u tamɬ~ɮaw, kamajanaj a pataħəkaɾ to~u miħamħamaj to~u ʦiɾo~umaʡaj; kawra o~u ro~uma i, paʡajawən paso~uwaɾ ŋaʡ tajra miɾiso~uʡ.
S.TRANSL: In some cultures, unexpected guests are welcome; in others, prior arrangements are preferred.

S.FORM: Kawra, oya cowa pidoko to Kawas a fa'inay mari'angay to fafahi, cowa ka "cicafay to fafahi".
Kawra, oya cowa pidoko to Kawas a fa'inay mari'angay to fafahi, cowa ka "cicafay to fafahi".
S.PHON: kawra, o~uja ʦo~uwa piɬ~ɮo~uko~u to~u kawas a faʡinaj mariʡaŋaj to~u fafaħi, ʦo~uwa ka "ʦiʦafaj to~u fafaħi".
S.TRANSL: Admittedly, there have been instances where an "unbelieving husband" seems to prove that he is not "agreeable to staying with her."

S.FORM: Wa misa'icel kita malakanga'ay cangraanan, kawra oha mamisinanot to sapatalipa'elal no saka 1 Korinto 15:33, edeng o maolahay ci Yihofaan a tamdaw ko hawidang.
Wa misa'icel kita malakanga'ay cangraanan, kawra oha mamisinanot to sapatalipa'elal no saka 1 Korinto 15:33, edeng o maolahay ci Yihofaan a tamdaw ko hawidang.
S.PHON: wa misaʡiʦəɾ kita maɾakaŋaʡaj ʦaŋraanan, kawra o~uħa mamisinano~ut to~u sapataɾipaʡəɾaɾ no~u saka * ko~urinto~u **:**, əɬ~ɮəŋ o~u mao~uɾaħaj ʦi jiħo~ufaan a tamɬ~ɮaw ko~u ħawiɬ~ɮaŋ.
S.TRANSL: In view of the clear warning at 1 Corinthians 15:33, we will have close friendships only with those who love Jehovah.

S.FORM: So'lin misanga'ay ci Aron to ekim a kolong, kawra so'linay a pasasiyor cingra a kalacafay to Lifi tamdaw midopo ci Yihofaan.
So'lin misanga'ay ci Aron to ekim a kolong, kawra so'linay a pasasiyor cingra a kalacafay to Lifi tamdaw midopo ci Yihofaan.
S.PHON: so~uʡɾin misaŋaʡaj ʦi aro~un to~u əkim a ko~uɾo~uŋ, kawra so~uʡɾinaj a pasasijo~ur ʦiŋra a kaɾaʦafaj to~u ɾifi tamɬ~ɮaw miɬ~ɮo~upo~u ʦi jiħo~ufaan.
S.TRANSL: Although initially involved in setting up the idol, Aaron repented and joined the rest of the Levites on Jehovah's side.

S.FORM: Ma'iwilayho kako ci Waltean, kawra misakafahay demak milowanay to somad no faloco'.
Ma'iwilayho kako ci Waltean, kawra misakafahay demak milowanay to somad no faloco'.
S.PHON: maʡiwiɾajħo~u kako~u ʦi waɾtəan, kawra misakafaħaj ɬ~ɮəmak miɾo~uwanaj to~u so~umaɬ~ɮ no~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ.
S.TRANSL: I do miss Walter very much, but pioneering helps me to cope.

S.FORM: Kawra 'alomanay a pingkosi cowa ka olah to tayal no niyah.
Kawra 'alomanay a pingkosi cowa ka olah to tayal no niyah.
S.PHON: kawra ʡaɾo~umanaj a piŋko~usi ʦo~uwa ka o~uɾaħ to~u tajaɾ no~u nijaħ.
S.TRANSL: "At my workplace in the bank, I now have much contact with lawyers.

S.FORM: Kawra, itini no ka'orip adihayay ko mami'afas to lipahak ita a demak, sasamaan kita nga' padadotoc a lipahak hani?
Kawra, itini no ka'orip adihayay ko mami'afas to lipahak ita a demak, sasamaan kita nga' padadotoc a lipahak hani?
S.PHON: kawra, itini no~u kaʡo~urip aɬ~ɮiħajaj ko~u mamiʡafas to~u ɾipaħak ita a ɬ~ɮəmak, sasamaan kita ŋaʡ paɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮo~uto~uʦ a ɾipaħak ħani?
S.TRANSL: But how can we be happy when there is so much that can rob us of happiness?

S.FORM: Kawra, halafinto i, mapili'ay kako to matiniay a noka'orip."
Kawra, halafinto i, mapili'ay kako to matiniay a noka'orip."
S.PHON: kawra, ħaɾafinto~u i, mapiɾiʡaj kako~u to~u matiniaj a no~ukaʡo~urip."
S.TRANSL: But the more I had sexual relations, the more insecure I felt."

S.FORM: So'lin o Kanata a tamdaw kako, kawra ono Afrika ko niyah sanay a miharateng.
So'lin o Kanata a tamdaw kako, kawra ono Afrika ko niyah sanay a miharateng.
S.PHON: so~uʡɾin o~u kanata a tamɬ~ɮaw kako~u, kawra o~uno~u afrika ko~u nijaħ sanaj a miħaratəŋ.
S.TRANSL: On paper we were Canadians, but we felt that we belonged in Africa.

S.FORM: Tada o ci'icelay ci Yis, kawra cowa ka macacoli ato tamdaw, nao hatiraay o sowal no Fangcalay Cudad "kari'angen no tao cingra i, pahater sa cingra.
Tada o ci'icelay ci Yis, kawra cowa ka macacoli ato tamdaw, nao hatiraay o sowal no Fangcalay Cudad "kari'angen no tao cingra i, pahater sa cingra.
S.PHON: taɬ~ɮa o~u ʦiʡiʦəɾaj ʦi jis, kawra ʦo~uwa ka maʦaʦo~uɾi ato~u tamɬ~ɮaw, nao~u ħatiraaj o~u so~uwaɾ no~u faŋʦaɾaj ʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ "kariʡaŋən no~u tao~u ʦiŋra i, paħatər sa ʦiŋra.
S.TRANSL: Yet, he did not get involved in fights, but "he let himself be afflicted," just as was foretold.

S.FORM: 'Ayaw, herek no canglah kinacecayay mirienang to taykay, kawra aninito folafoladan iraay ko taykay."
'Ayaw, herek no canglah kinacecayay mirienang to taykay, kawra aninito folafoladan iraay ko taykay."
S.PHON: ʡajaw, ħərək no~u ʦaŋɾaħ kinaʦəʦajaj miriənaŋ to~u tajkaj, kawra aninito~u fo~uɾafo~uɾaɬ~ɮan iraaj ko~u tajkaj."
S.TRANSL: Formerly all conventions were held during the late summer or the early fall; but now every month in the year has its conventions."

S.FORM: Itiya 80 ko mihecaan ni Mari, kawra mahemekay cingra mihayda.
Itiya 80 ko mihecaan ni Mari, kawra mahemekay cingra mihayda.
S.PHON: itija ** ko~u miħəʦaan ni mari, kawra maħəməkaj ʦiŋra miħajɬ~ɮa.
S.TRANSL: Though 80 years of age at the time, she gladly accepted the invitation.

S.FORM: Kawra, padotocay ci Alon mi'aca to so'linay kimad, mipili' malo Misawacayay ci Yihofaan i hakelong no ka'orip.
Kawra, padotocay ci Alon mi'aca to so'linay kimad, mipili' malo Misawacayay ci Yihofaan i hakelong no ka'orip.
S.PHON: kawra, paɬ~ɮo~uto~uʦaj ʦi aɾo~un miʡaʦa to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ, mipiɾiʡ maɾo~u misawaʦajaj ʦi jiħo~ufaan i ħakəɾo~uŋ no~u kaʡo~urip.
S.TRANSL: Undaunted, he continued to buy truth and served as a bold Witness of Jehovah for the rest of his life.

S.FORM: Salikorto, macadaayto ko tikami, kawra mapanilnilay miliso' to kasakopang!
Salikorto, macadaayto ko tikami, kawra mapanilnilay miliso' to kasakopang!
S.PHON: saɾiko~urto~u, maʦaɬ~ɮaajto~u ko~u tikami, kawra mapaniɾniɾaj miɾiso~uʡ to~u kasako~upaŋ!
S.TRANSL: Finally, a letter came one day, we were assigned to the traveling work!

S.FORM: So'li'lin misa'icelay ko wama wina pasifana' to wawa, kawra miliyasayho ko roma a wawa ci Yihofaan.
So'li'lin misa'icelay ko wama wina pasifana' to wawa, kawra miliyasayho ko roma a wawa ci Yihofaan.
S.PHON: so~uʡɾiʡɾin misaʡiʦəɾaj ko~u wama wina pasifanaʡ to~u wawa, kawra miɾijasajħo~u ko~u ro~uma a wawa ʦi jiħo~ufaan.
S.TRANSL: However, despite the parents' best efforts, some children leave Jehovah.

S.FORM: Iraay ko nga'ayay pinangan no roma tamdaw, kawra, salikor cowa pilifok to sapalemed no Kawas.
Iraay ko nga'ayay pinangan no roma tamdaw, kawra, salikor cowa pilifok to sapalemed no Kawas.
S.PHON: iraaj ko~u ŋaʡajaj pinaŋan no~u ro~uma tamɬ~ɮaw, kawra, saɾiko~ur ʦo~uwa piɾifo~uk to~u sapaɾəməɬ~ɮ no~u kawas.
S.TRANSL: Others who had admirable qualities failed to win God's approval.

S.FORM: Mato'asayto cingra, kawra miki'okan no Kawas to aaira ko fa'inayan a wawa, miharatengay cingra nao matayalay fafahiyan ko mamihofoc saan.
Mato'asayto cingra, kawra miki'okan no Kawas to aaira ko fa'inayan a wawa, miharatengay cingra nao matayalay fafahiyan ko mamihofoc saan.
S.PHON: mato~uʡasajto~u ʦiŋra, kawra mikiʡo~ukan no~u kawas to~u aaira ko~u faʡinajan a wawa, miħaratəŋaj ʦiŋra nao~u matajaɾaj fafaħijan ko~u mamiħo~ufo~uʦ saan.
S.TRANSL: Did aged Sarah expect that Jehovah would allow her to give birth to her own son, not just receive as son the child produced by her servant girl?

S.FORM: Kawra, o pakayraan no Loma to mapatayay tireng, kasimaan sanay ko demak, pakihater hananay mitating i tektek, anca saolatekay hananay a mitadem.
Kawra, o pakayraan no Loma to mapatayay tireng, kasimaan sanay ko demak, pakihater hananay mitating i tektek, anca saolatekay hananay a mitadem.
S.PHON: kawra, o~u pakajraan no~u ɾo~uma to~u mapatajaj tirəŋ, kasimaan sanaj ko~u ɬ~ɮəmak, pakiħatər ħananaj mitatiŋ i təktək, anʦa sao~uɾatəkaj ħananaj a mitaɬ~ɮəm.
S.TRANSL: As far as the Romans were concerned, though, the bodies of executed criminals either were left on the stake to rot or were thrown into a common grave.

S.FORM: So'lin mafana'ay ci Tewien tono kayki a demak, kawra pakayni haliepah, cowa ka halafin ko katayalan.
So'lin mafana'ay ci Tewien tono kayki a demak, kawra pakayni haliepah, cowa ka halafin ko katayalan.
S.PHON: so~uʡɾin mafanaʡaj ʦi təwiən to~uno~u kajki a ɬ~ɮəmak, kawra pakajni ħaɾiəpaħ, ʦo~uwa ka ħaɾafin ko~u katajaɾan.
S.TRANSL: Although Devynn was a skilled bookkeeper, his alcohol abuse prevented him from holding a permanent job.

S.FORM: Damsay ko sasowalen papadama kitanan mitafo to nga'ayay kalala'ed ato salawina, kawra ano makapah ko waywayan ita i, mangalefay o ciepocay.
Damsay ko sasowalen papadama kitanan mitafo to nga'ayay kalala'ed ato salawina, kawra ano makapah ko waywayan ita i, mangalefay o ciepocay.
S.PHON: ɬ~ɮamsaj ko~u saso~uwaɾən papaɬ~ɮama kitanan mitafo~u to~u ŋaʡajaj kaɾaɾaʡəɬ~ɮ ato~u saɾawina, kawra ano~u makapaħ ko~u wajwajan ita i, maŋaɾəfaj o~u ʦiəpo~uʦaj.
S.TRANSL: Although mild speech is helpful in dealing with opposing relatives, our good conduct can speak even louder.

S.FORM: Alatek masa'fit ko tamdaw, kawra cowa ko mamasa'fit ci Yihofa.
Alatek masa'fit ko tamdaw, kawra cowa ko mamasa'fit ci Yihofa.
S.PHON: aɾatək masaʡfit ko~u tamɬ~ɮaw, kawra ʦo~uwa ko~u mamasaʡfit ʦi jiħo~ufa.
S.TRANSL: Such hypocrisy might fool men, but not Jehovah.

S.FORM: Kawra cowa pihaen ci Apih, misamaanay hani?
Kawra cowa pihaen ci Apih, misamaanay hani?
S.PHON: kawra ʦo~uwa piħaən ʦi apiħ, misamaanaj ħani?
S.TRANSL: Why, then, does she always try to tell the truth?

S.FORM: Mahapinangay itini, isa'ayaway ko Kawas mirenec to no'adingoan a saali ita, kawra o papafeli to dafodafong i kitanan.
Mahapinangay itini, isa'ayaway ko Kawas mirenec to no'adingoan a saali ita, kawra o papafeli to dafodafong i kitanan.
S.PHON: maħapinaŋaj itini, isaʡajawaj ko~u kawas mirənəʦ to~u no~uʡaɬ~ɮiŋo~uan a saaɾi ita, kawra o~u papafəɾi to~u ɬ~ɮafo~uɬ~ɮafo~uŋ i kitanan.
S.TRANSL: God provides spiritual sustenance first, and then he satisfies our physical needs.

S.FORM: Latekay, sapi'acaw kita to so'linay kimad, wa misawad to 'akawangay ko lifon a tayal anca kamawmahan.
Latekay, sapi'acaw kita to so'linay kimad, wa misawad to 'akawangay ko lifon a tayal anca kamawmahan.
S.PHON: ɾatəkaj, sapiʡaʦaw kita to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ, wa misawaɬ~ɮ to~u ʡakawaŋaj ko~u ɾifo~un a tajaɾ anʦa kamawmaħan.
S.TRANSL: To buy truth, we might have to give up a lucrative job or career.

S.FORM: O mi'acaay to so'linay kimad a tamdaw wa misadak to toki.
O mi'acaay to so'linay kimad a tamdaw wa misadak to toki.
S.PHON: o~u miʡaʦaaj to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ a tamɬ~ɮaw wa misaɬ~ɮak to~u to~uki.
S.TRANSL: This is a price everyone who buys truth must pay.

S.FORM: 'Acaen ko So'linay Kimad, Hamaan Pa'acaen
'Acaen ko So'linay Kimad, Hamaan Pa'acaen
S.PHON: ʡaʦaən ko~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ, ħamaan paʡaʦaən
S.TRANSL: "Buy Truth and Never Sell It"

S.FORM: Misalimlaay kita toniya so'linay kimad, samanen o so'linay kimad papadama kitaanan miingid to misanga'ay a Tatapangan.
Misalimlaay kita toniya so'linay kimad, samanen o so'linay kimad papadama kitaanan miingid to misanga'ay a Tatapangan.
S.PHON: misaɾimɾaaj kita to~unija so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ, samanən o~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ papaɬ~ɮama kitaanan miiŋiɬ~ɮ to~u misaŋaʡaj a tatapaŋan.
S.TRANSL: We treasure these truths because they enable us to draw close to our Creator.

S.FORM: O mitilidan ni Yohani a tolo tikami adihayay ko kalimelaan a kimad.
O mitilidan ni Yohani a tolo tikami adihayay ko kalimelaan a kimad.
S.PHON: o~u mitiɾiɬ~ɮan ni jo~uħani a to~uɾo~u tikami aɬ~ɮiħajaj ko~u kaɾiməɾaan a kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: John's three letters contain further gems of truth.

S.FORM: Pakayni sapitengilaw to kimad, o fahka'ay a tamdaw macokacokay i fati'iyan no saso'ot a fiher, o roma tamdaw mikalicay i sapionto a kikay.
Pakayni sapitengilaw to kimad, o fahka'ay a tamdaw macokacokay i fati'iyan no saso'ot a fiher, o roma tamdaw mikalicay i sapionto a kikay.
S.PHON: pakajni sapitəŋiɾaw to~u kimaɬ~ɮ, o~u faħkaʡaj a tamɬ~ɮaw maʦo~ukaʦo~ukaj i fatiʡijan no~u saso~uʡo~ut a fiħər, o~u ro~uma tamɬ~ɮaw mikaɾiʦaj i sapio~unto~u a kikaj.
S.TRANSL: Some intrigued listeners hung from rope ladders on the sides of the gymnasium; others perched atop the exercise equipment.

S.FORM: Dotocto wa milengo Pakayni sapi'acaaw to so'linay kimad, wa pasadak kita to maanan a lima sapi'aca?
Dotocto wa milengo Pakayni sapi'acaaw to so'linay kimad, wa pasadak kita to maanan a lima sapi'aca?
S.PHON: ɬ~ɮo~uto~uʦto~u wa miɾəŋo~u pakajni sapiʡaʦaaw to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ, wa pasaɬ~ɮak kita to~u maanan a ɾima sapiʡaʦa?
S.TRANSL: So, then, let us consider five things that we may have to pay to buy truth.

S.FORM: Pasifana' Kita to So'linay Kimad
Pasifana' Kita to So'linay Kimad
S.PHON: pasifanaʡ kita to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ
S.TRANSL: Teaching the Truth

S.FORM: So'linay kimad a safaked misamaan midipot kitaanan?
So'linay kimad a safaked misamaan midipot kitaanan?
S.PHON: so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ a safakəɬ~ɮ misamaan miɬ~ɮipo~ut kitaanan?
S.TRANSL: How does the belt of truth protect us?

S.FORM: O sowal no iraay itiya a tamdaw, pakayniay to matiniay a toki minanam to so'linay kimad ko 'alomanay tamdaw sanay.
O sowal no iraay itiya a tamdaw, pakayniay to matiniay a toki minanam to so'linay kimad ko 'alomanay tamdaw sanay.
S.PHON: o~u so~uwaɾ no~u iraaj itija a tamɬ~ɮaw, pakajniaj to~u matiniaj a to~uki minanam to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ ko~u ʡaɾo~umanaj tamɬ~ɮaw sanaj.
S.TRANSL: According to eyewitness accounts, many learned the truth in this way.

S.FORM: Oniya tosa lalakaw pakalalokay kitaanan misalimla to so'linay kimad.
Oniya tosa lalakaw pakalalokay kitaanan misalimla to so'linay kimad.
S.PHON: o~unija to~usa ɾaɾakaw pakaɾaɾo~ukaj kitaanan misaɾimɾa to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: These two articles encourage us to appreciate the priceless value of Jehovah's precious truth.

S.FORM: Pakayni sapi'acaaw to so'linay kimad, wa pasadak kita to maanan a lima sapi'aca?
Pakayni sapi'acaaw to so'linay kimad, wa pasadak kita to maanan a lima sapi'aca?
S.PHON: pakajni sapiʡaʦaaw to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ, wa pasaɬ~ɮak kita to~u maanan a ɾima sapiʡaʦa?
S.TRANSL: Describe five things that may be included in the price we have to pay for obtaining truth.

S.FORM: Nano kaemangho pasifana'anto no wama to kimad, pakarongayho micadaayto to ino'.
Nano kaemangho pasifana'anto no wama to kimad, pakarongayho micadaayto to ino'.
S.PHON: nano~u kaəmaŋħo~u pasifanaʡanto~u no~u wama to~u kimaɬ~ɮ, pakaro~uŋajħo~u miʦaɬ~ɮaajto~u to~u ino~uʡ.
S.TRANSL: They had been taught about Jehovah from infancy by their father and got baptized as teenagers.

S.FORM: Ikor no 30 miheca, kamayan miemin to toki a mitosil ci Miriyana, malacafay to fa'inay a padama to tamdaw minanam to so'linay kimad.
Ikor no 30 miheca, kamayan miemin to toki a mitosil ci Miriyana, malacafay to fa'inay a padama to tamdaw minanam to so'linay kimad.
S.PHON: iko~ur no~u ** miħəʦa, kamajan miəmin to~u to~uki a mito~usiɾ ʦi mirijana, maɾaʦafaj to~u faʡinaj a paɬ~ɮama to~u tamɬ~ɮaw minanam to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Over 30 years later, Mirjana is still in full-time service, together with her husband.

S.FORM: Saka tengilaw no 'alomanay to kimad ningra, tomirengay i cokowi ci Rosofo a mikimad.
Saka tengilaw no 'alomanay to kimad ningra, tomirengay i cokowi ci Rosofo a mikimad.
S.PHON: saka təŋiɾaw no~u ʡaɾo~umanaj to~u kimaɬ~ɮ niŋra, to~umirəŋaj i ʦo~uko~uwi ʦi ro~uso~ufo~u a mikimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: But Brother Rutherford kept his poise and calmly climbed onto a table to make himself heard.

S.FORM: O So'linay Kimad, "Caay ko Pararihadayay, O Pafangafangay"
O So'linay Kimad, "Caay ko Pararihadayay, O Pafangafangay"
S.PHON: o~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ, "ʦaaj ko~u parariħaɬ~ɮajaj, o~u pafaŋafaŋaj"
S.TRANSL: The Truth Brings, "Not Peace, But a Sword"

S.FORM: Pakapapotalay Kimad Mitenakay to Nga'ayay a Lihaf i Aielan
Pakapapotalay Kimad Mitenakay to Nga'ayay a Lihaf i Aielan
S.PHON: pakapapo~utaɾaj kimaɬ~ɮ mitənakaj to~u ŋaʡajaj a ɾiħaf i aiəɾan
S.TRANSL: Public Talks Spread the Good News in Ireland

S.FORM: O mitilidan ni Yohani saka tosa ato saka tolo a tikami, pakahemekan ningra koya padotocay 'midoedo to so'linay kimad dademak' a Krisciyang.
O mitilidan ni Yohani saka tosa ato saka tolo a tikami, pakahemekan ningra koya padotocay 'midoedo to so'linay kimad dademak' a Krisciyang.
S.PHON: o~u mitiɾiɬ~ɮan ni jo~uħani saka to~usa ato~u saka to~uɾo~u a tikami, pakaħəməkan niŋra ko~uja paɬ~ɮo~uto~uʦaj ʡmiɬ~ɮo~uəɬ~ɮo~u to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmakʡ a krisʦijaŋ.
S.TRANSL: His second and third letters commend Christians who continue "walking in the truth."

S.FORM: Mikilimay to kimad
Mikilimay to kimad
S.PHON: mikiɾimaj to~u kimaɬ~ɮ
S.TRANSL: Honest hearts he surely knows,

S.FORM: Wa nga'ay kita mikitini mifekaday a demak tiya miliyas ko Israil tamdaw to Icip caka halafin a minanam to kalimlaan a kimad.
Wa nga'ay kita mikitini mifekaday a demak tiya miliyas ko Israil tamdaw to Icip caka halafin a minanam to kalimlaan a kimad.
S.PHON: wa ŋaʡaj kita mikitini mifəkaɬ~ɮaj a ɬ~ɮəmak tija miɾijas ko~u israiɾ tamɬ~ɮaw to~u iʦip ʦaka ħaɾafin a minanam to~u kaɾimɾaan a kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Additional lessons can be learned from what took place shortly after the Israelites were freed from Egypt.

S.FORM: Tona misalimelaay kita to Kimad no Kawas, miharatengay to sapilayapaw to epoc naitira.
Tona misalimelaay kita to Kimad no Kawas, miharatengay to sapilayapaw to epoc naitira.
S.PHON: to~una misaɾiməɾaaj kita to~u kimaɬ~ɮ no~u kawas, miħaratəŋaj to~u sapiɾajapaw to~u əpo~uʦ naitira.
S.TRANSL: It is only sensible to conclude that because we appreciate God's Word, we will want to derive full benefit from it.

S.FORM: Tedian no kimad,
Tedian no kimad,
S.PHON: təɬ~ɮian no~u kimaɬ~ɮ,
S.TRANSL: Sparkling like the daylight,

S.FORM: Toniya matiliday, patinakoan ni Yihofa ko kimad Ningra to nanom, hacol, epah.
Toniya matiliday, patinakoan ni Yihofa ko kimad Ningra to nanom, hacol, epah.
S.PHON: to~unija matiɾiɬ~ɮaj, patinako~uan ni jiħo~ufa ko~u kimaɬ~ɮ niŋra to~u nano~um, ħaʦo~uɾ, əpaħ.
S.TRANSL: In this Bible passage, Jehovah compares his word to water, milk, and wine.

S.FORM: Ano saka cipinangaw toniya kalimlaan a kimad i, salakat folfolen ita o maan ko lalangian no tamdaw milifok to so'lin a lipahak.
Ano saka cipinangaw toniya kalimlaan a kimad i, salakat folfolen ita o maan ko lalangian no tamdaw milifok to so'lin a lipahak.
S.PHON: ano~u saka ʦipinaŋaw to~unija kaɾimɾaan a kimaɬ~ɮ i, saɾakat fo~uɾfo~uɾən ita o~u maan ko~u ɾaɾaŋian no~u tamɬ~ɮaw miɾifo~uk to~u so~uʡɾin a ɾipaħak.
S.TRANSL: We can understand this vital truth more fully if we discern what causes inner joy and what does not.

S.FORM: O roma tamdaw mirienangay to kapot no misafelitay to kimad a tamdaw.
O roma tamdaw mirienangay to kapot no misafelitay to kimad a tamdaw.
S.PHON: o~u ro~uma tamɬ~ɮaw miriənaŋaj to~u kapo~ut no~u misafəɾitaj to~u kimaɬ~ɮ a tamɬ~ɮaw.
S.TRANSL: Still others took sides with apostates and other opposers who misrepresented our beliefs.

S.FORM: Misafelitay to kimad tamdaw ato micoliay a tamdaw mialaay to mitepangan no mita a lalakaw pasadak i wangcan nangra, mihaen o sapisolotaw to Misawacayay Tamdaw ni Yihofa ato romaroma tamdaw tayra misolap i wangcan nangra.

Misafelitay to kimad tamdaw ato micoliay a tamdaw mialaay to mitepangan no mita a lalakaw pasadak i wangcan nangra, mihaen o sapisolotaw to Misawacayay Tamdaw ni Yihofa ato romaroma tamdaw tayra misolap i wangcan nangra.
S.PHON: misafəɾitaj to~u kimaɬ~ɮ tamɬ~ɮaw ato~u miʦo~uɾiaj a tamɬ~ɮaw miaɾaaj to~u mitəpaŋan no~u mita a ɾaɾakaw pasaɬ~ɮak i waŋʦan naŋra, miħaən o~u sapiso~uɾo~utaw to~u misawaʦajaj tamɬ~ɮaw ni jiħo~ufa ato~u ro~umaro~uma tamɬ~ɮaw tajra miso~uɾap i waŋʦan naŋra.
S.TRANSL: Apostates and other opposers try to use our publications on their websites to lure in Jehovah's Witnesses and others.

S.FORM: O roma tamdaw samaanay palidlid sanay a miliyas to so'linay kimad?
O roma tamdaw samaanay palidlid sanay a miliyas to so'linay kimad?
S.PHON: o~u ro~uma tamɬ~ɮaw samaanaj paɾiɬ~ɮɾiɬ~ɮ sanaj a miɾijas to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ?
S.TRANSL: How might one gradually leave the truth?

S.FORM: Ano sapidoedoaw kita to so'linay kimad a midemak, wa micada ato midofdof to kihcod a limo'ot ni Yihofa.
Ano sapidoedoaw kita to so'linay kimad a midemak, wa micada ato midofdof to kihcod a limo'ot ni Yihofa.
S.PHON: ano~u sapiɬ~ɮo~uəɬ~ɮo~uaw kita to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ a miɬ~ɮəmak, wa miʦaɬ~ɮa ato~u miɬ~ɮo~ufɬ~ɮo~uf to~u kiħʦo~uɬ~ɮ a ɾimo~uʡo~ut ni jiħo~ufa.
S.TRANSL: To walk in the truth, we must accept and obey all of Jehovah's sayings.

S.FORM: Pakayni tisowanan hano kalimlaan ko so'linay kimad hani?
Pakayni tisowanan hano kalimlaan ko so'linay kimad hani?
S.PHON: pakajni tiso~uwanan ħano~u kaɾimɾaan ko~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ ħani?
S.TRANSL: Why is the truth precious to you?

S.FORM: Tiya milimasmas ci Yangke to kimad ni Rosofo i, o sofitay ko mikowanay, samanen iraay ko mamitowakang to mirikecay.
Tiya milimasmas ci Yangke to kimad ni Rosofo i, o sofitay ko mikowanay, samanen iraay ko mamitowakang to mirikecay.
S.PHON: tija miɾimasmas ʦi jaŋkə to~u kimaɬ~ɮ ni ro~uso~ufo~u i, o~u so~ufitaj ko~u miko~uwanaj, samanən iraaj ko~u mamito~uwakaŋ to~u mirikəʦaj.
S.TRANSL: Just as Brother Young made arrangements for Brother Rutherford's talk, the government imposed martial law because of an attempted coup d'état.

S.FORM: Sasamaan ko pinengneng ita to so'linay kimad?
Sasamaan ko pinengneng ita to so'linay kimad?
S.PHON: sasamaan ko~u pinəŋnəŋ ita to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ?
S.TRANSL: How valuable should the truth be to us?

S.FORM: Misatapangay maolah ko wina to so'linay kimad.
Misatapangay maolah ko wina to so'linay kimad.
S.PHON: misatapaŋaj mao~uɾaħ ko~u wina to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: This was the beginning of my mother's interest in the truth.

S.FORM: Saka tosa romi'ad, O Sikolo a singpon no Potawya masadakay ko kimad ni Rosofo.
Saka tosa romi'ad, O Sikolo a singpon no Potawya masadakay ko kimad ni Rosofo.
S.PHON: saka to~usa ro~umiʡaɬ~ɮ, o~u siko~uɾo~u a siŋpo~un no~u po~utawja masaɬ~ɮakaj ko~u kimaɬ~ɮ ni ro~uso~ufo~u.
S.TRANSL: The very next day, the newspaper O Século published an article about Brother Rutherford's lecture.

S.FORM: O roma Krisciyang hano pa'aca to so'linay kimad?
O roma Krisciyang hano pa'aca to so'linay kimad?
S.PHON: o~u ro~uma krisʦijaŋ ħano~u paʡaʦa to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ?
S.TRANSL: What could cause a Christian to "sell" the truth?

S.FORM: Cekiwen ko kimad a midofdof,
Cekiwen ko kimad a midofdof,
S.PHON: ʦəkiwən ko~u kimaɬ~ɮ a miɬ~ɮo~ufɬ~ɮo~uf,
S.TRANSL: Searching the Scriptures, we hear your command;

S.FORM: Ano saka dademaken ita ko mitenakan no niyah a so'linay kimad, wa madoloto a miliyas to talakal ni Satan.
Ano saka dademaken ita ko mitenakan no niyah a so'linay kimad, wa madoloto a miliyas to talakal ni Satan.
S.PHON: ano~u saka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmakən ita ko~u mitənakan no~u nijaħ a so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ, wa maɬ~ɮo~uɾo~uto~u a miɾijas to~u taɾakaɾ ni satan.
S.TRANSL: The more our speech and actions harmonize, the less likely we are to be caught in Satan's snares.

S.FORM: So'lin caka hakowa ko maolahay to so'linay kimad a tamdaw, samaan kiso milifok to hemek i pitosilan hani?
So'lin caka hakowa ko maolahay to so'linay kimad a tamdaw, samaan kiso milifok to hemek i pitosilan hani?
S.PHON: so~uʡɾin ʦaka ħako~uwa ko~u mao~uɾaħaj to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ a tamɬ~ɮaw, samaan kiso~u miɾifo~uk to~u ħəmək i pito~usiɾan ħani?
S.TRANSL: Even if few people respond favorably to the good news, how can you find pleasure in your ministry?

S.FORM: Ikor no pina miheca, misa'icelay ko tarokos ci Pawlo toniya so'linay kimad, ano palada' kitinien i matatodongay a araaw ko pidemak.
Ikor no pina miheca, misa'icelay ko tarokos ci Pawlo toniya so'linay kimad, ano palada' kitinien i matatodongay a araaw ko pidemak.
S.PHON: iko~ur no~u pina miħəʦa, misaʡiʦəɾaj ko~u taro~uko~us ʦi pawɾo~u to~unija so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ, ano~u paɾaɬ~ɮaʡ kitiniən i matato~uɬ~ɮo~uŋaj a araaw ko~u piɬ~ɮəmak.
S.TRANSL: Some years later, the apostle Paul emphasized the same important truth about having the right motive in giving.

S.FORM: O Misawacayay Tamdaw ni Yihofa ci Wili, tano kacekidan a pakayraan ko sapasifana' ningra to wina to so'linay kimad.
O Misawacayay Tamdaw ni Yihofa ci Wili, tano kacekidan a pakayraan ko sapasifana' ningra to wina to so'linay kimad.
S.PHON: o~u misawaʦajaj tamɬ~ɮaw ni jiħo~ufa ʦi wiɾi, tano~u kaʦəkiɬ~ɮan a pakajraan ko~u sapasifanaʡ niŋra to~u wina to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Willie was one of Jehovah's Witnesses, and this was my mother's shocking introduction to Bible truth.

S.FORM: O nalifetan ni Iciyang mitadtaday to maanan kalimelaan a kimad hani?
O nalifetan ni Iciyang mitadtaday to maanan kalimelaan a kimad hani?
S.PHON: o~u naɾifətan ni iʦijaŋ mitaɬ~ɮtaɬ~ɮaj to~u maanan kaɾiməɾaan a kimaɬ~ɮ ħani?
S.TRANSL: What truth does Ida's experience highlight?

S.FORM: Songila'en ita misaka dademak ko toki, hoeca piliyas to so'linay kimad.
Songila'en ita misaka dademak ko toki, hoeca piliyas to so'linay kimad.
S.PHON: so~uŋiɾaʡən ita misaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmak ko~u to~uki, ħo~uəʦa piɾijas to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: To avoid drifting away from the truth, we must make wise use of our time.

S.FORM: Samaan ko demak ita cato ka piliyas to so'linay kimad anca 'mapawpaway a ma'alol?
Samaan ko demak ita cato ka piliyas to so'linay kimad anca 'mapawpaway a ma'alol?
S.PHON: samaan ko~u ɬ~ɮəmak ita ʦato~u ka piɾijas to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ anʦa ʡmapawpawaj a maʡaɾo~uɾ?
S.TRANSL: How can we avoid either drawing away from or drifting away from the truth?

S.FORM: O romaroma matedalayto a Misawacayay Tamdaw ni Yihofa ikor tayniay i Kirekistan, saka mihaenay a matenak ko so'linay kimad.
O romaroma matedalayto a Misawacayay Tamdaw ni Yihofa ikor tayniay i Kirekistan, saka mihaenay a matenak ko so'linay kimad.
S.PHON: o~u ro~umaro~uma matəɬ~ɮaɾajto~u a misawaʦajaj tamɬ~ɮaw ni jiħo~ufa iko~ur tajniaj i kirəkistan, saka miħaənaj a matənak ko~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: When these exiles were released, many came to Kyrgyzstan, and some of them brought the truth with them.

S.FORM: Maolahay to mitosilay demak ci Pawlo, mi'ametay to toki mitenak to kimad.
Maolahay to mitosilay demak ci Pawlo, mi'ametay to toki mitenak to kimad.
S.PHON: mao~uɾaħaj to~u mito~usiɾaj ɬ~ɮəmak ʦi pawɾo~u, miʡamətaj to~u to~uki mitənak to~u kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: One way that Paul demonstrated appreciation for the ministry was by being alert to opportunities to speak to others.

S.FORM: Ano sapadotocaw midofdof to so'linay kimad a midemak kita i, wa matatodong ko pinengneng to hafay nga'.
Ano sapadotocaw midofdof to so'linay kimad a midemak kita i, wa matatodong ko pinengneng to hafay nga'.
S.PHON: ano~u sapaɬ~ɮo~uto~uʦaw miɬ~ɮo~ufɬ~ɮo~uf to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ a miɬ~ɮəmak kita i, wa matato~uɬ~ɮo~uŋ ko~u pinəŋnəŋ to~u ħafaj ŋaʡ.
S.TRANSL: If we are to continue walking in the truth, we must keep material things in their proper place.

S.FORM: Tinakoen mitadtad to Matay 11:28 to 30, o so'li'linay a kimad konini.
Tinakoen mitadtad to Matay 11:28 to 30, o so'li'linay a kimad konini.
S.PHON: tinako~uən mitaɬ~ɮtaɬ~ɮ to~u mataj **:** to~u **, o~u so~uʡɾiʡɾinaj a kimaɬ~ɮ ko~unini.
S.TRANSL: Relate an experience that illustrates the truthfulness of Matthew 11:28-30.

S.FORM: Saka dademaken ko so'linay kimad wa milifok to maanan a epoc hani?
Saka dademaken ko so'linay kimad wa milifok to maanan a epoc hani?
S.PHON: saka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmakən ko~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ wa miɾifo~uk to~u maanan a əpo~uʦ ħani?
S.TRANSL: Illustrate. b. How does truth benefit us?

S.FORM: Dopoen kimad.
Dopoen kimad.
S.PHON: ɬ~ɮo~upo~uən kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Truth we will maintain.

S.FORM: Saka tosa, mitolon kita milongoc to Kawas padama toniya tamdaw makahi micada to so'linay kimad.
Saka tosa, mitolon kita milongoc to Kawas padama toniya tamdaw makahi micada to so'linay kimad.
S.PHON: saka to~usa, mito~uɾo~un kita miɾo~uŋo~uʦ to~u kawas paɬ~ɮama to~unija tamɬ~ɮaw makaħi miʦaɬ~ɮa to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Second, we supplicate Jehovah in prayer to open the heart of sincere ones.

S.FORM: Oniya mararamoday laloma' no 5 lipay malaheciay nangra mi'osi ko Mala'osiay a Mahimeday, ikor micadaay to so'linay a kimad.
Oniya mararamoday laloma' no 5 lipay malaheciay nangra mi'osi ko Mala'osiay a Mahimeday, ikor micadaay to so'linay a kimad.
S.PHON: o~unija mararamo~uɬ~ɮaj ɾaɾo~umaʡ no~u * ɾipaj maɾaħəʦiaj naŋra miʡo~usi ko~u maɾaʡo~usiaj a maħiməɬ~ɮaj, iko~ur miʦaɬ~ɮaaj to~u so~uʡɾinaj a kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: F. Crist, a traveling overseer in Canada, told of a couple who had read The Finished Mystery and accepted the truth in only five weeks!

S.FORM: Kamayan 'aloman ko tamdaw maolahay to so'linay kimad no Fangcalay Cudad.
Kamayan 'aloman ko tamdaw maolahay to so'linay kimad no Fangcalay Cudad.
S.PHON: kamajan ʡaɾo~uman ko~u tamɬ~ɮaw mao~uɾaħaj to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ no~u faŋʦaɾaj ʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Honesthearted ones were still responding to the good news.

S.FORM: Samaan ko demak ita nga' eca ka pa'aca to so'linay kimad?
Samaan ko demak ita nga' eca ka pa'aca to so'linay kimad?
S.PHON: samaan ko~u ɬ~ɮəmak ita ŋaʡ əʦa ka paʡaʦa to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ?
S.TRANSL: What initial step will help us to avoid selling the truth?

S.FORM: O epoc no so'linay kimad mata'lifay ko misadakan ita a sapi'aca.
O epoc no so'linay kimad mata'lifay ko misadakan ita a sapi'aca.
S.PHON: o~u əpo~uʦ no~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ mataʡɾifaj ko~u misaɬ~ɮakan ita a sapiʡaʦa.
S.TRANSL: The value of that truth goes far beyond any sacrifice we make to obtain it.

S.FORM: mitooray to kimad.
mitooray to kimad.
S.PHON: mito~uo~uraj to~u kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Who handle the truth aright.

S.FORM: Wa mahapinangho ita, sapi'acaaw to so'linay kimad a mihadakan to sapi'aca tada ciepocay.
Wa mahapinangho ita, sapi'acaaw to so'linay kimad a mihadakan to sapi'aca tada ciepocay.
S.PHON: wa maħapinaŋħo~u ita, sapiʡaʦaaw to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ a miħaɬ~ɮakan to~u sapiʡaʦa taɬ~ɮa ʦiəpo~uʦaj.
S.TRANSL: As we will see, buying truth is well worth the cost.

S.FORM: Nengneng ko kamok no JW Tilifi, pili'en ko MALICAYAY ATO NALIFETAN, MIFALICAY TO KA'ORIP KO SO'LINAY KIMAD.
Nengneng ko kamok no JW Tilifi, pili'en ko MALICAYAY ATO NALIFETAN, MIFALICAY TO KA'ORIP KO SO'LINAY KIMAD.
S.PHON: nəŋnəŋ ko~u kamo~uk no~u *w tiɾifi, piɾiʡən ko~u maɾiʦajaj ato~u naɾifətan, mifaɾiʦaj to~u kaʡo~urip ko~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Go to JW Broadcasting, and look under INTERVIEWS AND EXPERIENCES, TRUTH TRANSFORMS LIVES.

S.FORM: Safalod nga'ayay o ta'enengan no so'linay kimad anca o tatooren a palinian ko tatodong, nga'ayay sapiharateng anca o taadoan a midemak.
Safalod nga'ayay o ta'enengan no so'linay kimad anca o tatooren a palinian ko tatodong, nga'ayay sapiharateng anca o taadoan a midemak.
S.PHON: safaɾo~uɬ~ɮ ŋaʡajaj o~u taʡənəŋan no~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ anʦa o~u tato~uo~urən a paɾinian ko~u tato~uɬ~ɮo~uŋ, ŋaʡajaj sapiħaratəŋ anʦa o~u taaɬ~ɮo~uan a miɬ~ɮəmak.
S.TRANSL: A principle is a fundamental truth or doctrine that is used as a basis of reasoning or action.

S.FORM: Mihapinang to pakaynian ni Yihofa i Kimad midama ko tamdaw Ningra?
Mihapinang to pakaynian ni Yihofa i Kimad midama ko tamdaw Ningra?
S.PHON: miħapinaŋ to~u pakajnian ni jiħo~ufa i kimaɬ~ɮ miɬ~ɮama ko~u tamɬ~ɮaw niŋra?
S.TRANSL: see how Jehovah sustains his people with his Word?

S.FORM: O hatiniay mi'osiay awaay ko kafana'an to kimad no Kristokiyo aletek, cowa pakaso'lin to pihakelongan anca cowa ka fana' to epoc no Fangcalay Cudad i tamdaw.
O hatiniay mi'osiay awaay ko kafana'an to kimad no Kristokiyo aletek, cowa pakaso'lin to pihakelongan anca cowa ka fana' to epoc no Fangcalay Cudad i tamdaw.
S.PHON: o~u ħatiniaj miʡo~usiaj awaaj ko~u kafanaʡan to~u kimaɬ~ɮ no~u kristo~ukijo~u aɾətək, ʦo~uwa pakaso~uʡɾin to~u piħakəɾo~uŋan anʦa ʦo~uwa ka fanaʡ to~u əpo~uʦ no~u faŋʦaɾaj ʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ i tamɬ~ɮaw.
S.TRANSL: They may know nothing about Christian teachings, they may be somewhat distrustful of religion, or they may be unaware that the Bible has practical value.

S.FORM: O sapasifana' ni Yis a kimad samaanay mikelec kitanan a malacecay hani?
O sapasifana' ni Yis a kimad samaanay mikelec kitanan a malacecay hani?
S.PHON: o~u sapasifanaʡ ni jis a kimaɬ~ɮ samaanaj mikəɾəʦ kitanan a maɾaʦəʦaj ħani?
S.TRANSL: And how will his teaching help us to keep united?

S.FORM: O Krisciyang sasamaan "mi'aca to so'linay kimad"?
O Krisciyang sasamaan "mi'aca to so'linay kimad"?
S.PHON: o~u krisʦijaŋ sasamaan "miʡaʦa to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ"?
S.TRANSL: How can a Christian "buy truth," as urged at Proverbs 23:23?

S.FORM: Mihaenay a micerid "tano so'linay kimad misafaked to tatelecan" tada o ciepocay!
Mihaenay a micerid "tano so'linay kimad misafaked to tatelecan" tada o ciepocay!
S.PHON: miħaənaj a miʦəriɬ~ɮ "tano~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ misafakəɬ~ɮ to~u tatəɾəʦan" taɬ~ɮa o~u ʦiəpo~uʦaj!
S.TRANSL: Certainly, it is worth keeping "the belt of truth fastened around your waist" at all times.

S.FORM: Itini pitosilan samaan kita misaka dademak to "Lalosidan no Sapasifana'" pasifana' to tamdaw to so'linay kimad?
Itini pitosilan samaan kita misaka dademak to "Lalosidan no Sapasifana'" pasifana' to tamdaw to so'linay kimad?
S.PHON: itini pito~usiɾan samaan kita misaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmak to~u "ɾaɾo~usiɬ~ɮan no~u sapasifanaʡ" pasifanaʡ to~u tamɬ~ɮaw to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ?
S.TRANSL: How can we use our Teaching Toolbox to teach the truth in our ministry?

S.FORM: Tokel ni Yihofa ano mamisa'osi kita to nga'ayay tatiihay i, micada to sakayat no Kimad Ningra.
Tokel ni Yihofa ano mamisa'osi kita to nga'ayay tatiihay i, micada to sakayat no Kimad Ningra.
S.PHON: to~ukəɾ ni jiħo~ufa ano~u mamisaʡo~usi kita to~u ŋaʡajaj tatiiħaj i, miʦaɬ~ɮa to~u sakajat no~u kimaɬ~ɮ niŋra.
S.TRANSL: When it comes to issues of right and wrong, Jehovah wants us to be guided by his thinking as revealed in his Word.

S.FORM: Na somowalay ci Yis, papadama ko fangcalay saocor kitanan pakaharateng to mananamayto ita a so'linay kimad.
Na somowalay ci Yis, papadama ko fangcalay saocor kitanan pakaharateng to mananamayto ita a so'linay kimad.
S.PHON: na so~umo~uwaɾaj ʦi jis, papaɬ~ɮama ko~u faŋʦaɾaj sao~uʦo~ur kitanan pakaħaratəŋ to~u mananamajto~u ita a so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Jesus taught that God's spirit can bring back to our mind things that we have learned.

S.FORM: Ora tayraay a fa'inayan hato fohat sanay ko faloco', cipinangto to so'linay kimad no Fangcalay Cudad.
Ora tayraay a fa'inayan hato fohat sanay ko faloco', cipinangto to so'linay kimad no Fangcalay Cudad.
S.PHON: o~ura tajraaj a faʡinajan ħato~u fo~uħat sanaj ko~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ, ʦipinaŋto~u to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ no~u faŋʦaɾaj ʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Even without the use of sound equipment, the speaker's voice carries to every part of the hall, holding the audience's attention for one and a half hours.

S.FORM: Mahecaday kiso ci Ciraan mifohat to faloco' a micawma' to cowa ka lecad a widang, wa ma'araw ko 'icel no Kimad ato fangcalay saocor no Kawas, padama to Krisciyang makelec a malacecay.
Mahecaday kiso ci Ciraan mifohat to faloco' a micawma' to cowa ka lecad a widang, wa ma'araw ko 'icel no Kimad ato fangcalay saocor no Kawas, padama to Krisciyang makelec a malacecay.
S.PHON: maħəʦaɬ~ɮaj kiso~u ʦi ʦiraan mifo~uħat to~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ a miʦawmaʡ to~u ʦo~uwa ka ɾəʦaɬ~ɮ a wiɬ~ɮaŋ, wa maʡaraw ko~u ʡiʦəɾ no~u kimaɬ~ɮ ato~u faŋʦaɾaj sao~uʦo~ur no~u kawas, paɬ~ɮama to~u krisʦijaŋ makəɾəʦ a maɾaʦəʦaj.
S.TRANSL: When you broaden out in this way, you will see clear evidence of the uniting power of God's Word and spirit.

S.FORM: I satapang pangihaen ko radiw misolot to tamdaw mingata, ikor nga' pangiha to kimad no Fangcalay Cudad.
I satapang pangihaen ko radiw misolot to tamdaw mingata, ikor nga' pangiha to kimad no Fangcalay Cudad.
S.PHON: i satapaŋ paŋiħaən ko~u raɬ~ɮiw miso~uɾo~ut to~u tamɬ~ɮaw miŋata, iko~ur ŋaʡ paŋiħa to~u kimaɬ~ɮ no~u faŋʦaɾaj ʦo~u~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Usually, a music recording would be played first to get the people's attention, and then a recorded Bible lecture would be played.

S.FORM: Pakayni piparatoh, mikitini Kimad no Kawas, wa mananam ita ko maanan hani?"
Pakayni piparatoh, mikitini Kimad no Kawas, wa mananam ita ko maanan hani?"
S.PHON: pakajni piparato~uħ, mikitini kimaɬ~ɮ no~u kawas, wa mananam ita ko~u maanan ħani?"
S.TRANSL: And what can God's Word teach me about greetings?'

S.FORM: Saka dademaken ita ko mananamay a so'linay kimad, papalemeden ni Yihofa kita.
Saka dademaken ita ko mananamay a so'linay kimad, papalemeden ni Yihofa kita.
S.PHON: saka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmakən ita ko~u mananamaj a so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ, papaɾəməɬ~ɮən ni jiħo~ufa kita.
S.TRANSL: Doing what we know to be right brings rich blessings.

S.FORM: Mihaenay, songila'en misaka dademak ko toki, mi'aca to so'linay kimad.
Mihaenay, songila'en misaka dademak ko toki, mi'aca to so'linay kimad.
S.PHON: miħaənaj, so~uŋiɾaʡən misaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmak ko~u to~uki, miʡaʦa to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ.
S.TRANSL: Today, the important thing is that we use our time wisely so as to buy as much truth as our circumstances allow.

S.FORM: Sasamaan ko demak ita nga' matongal ko olah a minanam to kimad no Kawas hani?
Sasamaan ko demak ita nga' matongal ko olah a minanam to kimad no Kawas hani?
S.PHON: sasamaan ko~u ɬ~ɮəmak ita ŋaʡ mato~uŋaɾ ko~u o~uɾaħ a minanam to~u kimaɬ~ɮ no~u kawas ħani?
S.TRANSL: How can we become better students of God's Word?

S.FORM: Mitosil ci Pawlo i, tahalitemohay to tangongol ko pakaso'lin to mararaway a kimad a tamdaw.
Mitosil ci Pawlo i, tahalitemohay to tangongol ko pakaso'lin to mararaway a kimad a tamdaw.
S.PHON: mito~usiɾ ʦi pawɾo~u i, taħaɾitəmo~uħaj to~u taŋo~uŋo~uɾ ko~u pakaso~uʡɾin to~u mararawaj a kimaɬ~ɮ a tamɬ~ɮaw.
S.TRANSL: At times, Paul encountered strongly entrenched false beliefs.

S.FORM: Sapadotocaw kita midoedo to so'linay kimad a midemak, oha mamisa'icel eca ka padik no saroero.
Sapadotocaw kita midoedo to so'linay kimad a midemak, oha mamisa'icel eca ka padik no saroero.
S.PHON: sapaɬ~ɮo~uto~uʦaw kita miɬ~ɮo~uəɬ~ɮo~u to~u so~uʡɾinaj kimaɬ~ɮ a miɬ~ɮəmak, o~uħa mamisaʡiʦəɾ əʦa ka paɬ~ɮik no~u saro~uəro~u.
S.TRANSL: To continue walking in the truth, we must not give in to pressure from others.

S.FORM: Tada ci'icelayto ko kimad no Kawas!
Tada ci'icelayto ko kimad no Kawas!
S.PHON: taɬ~ɮa ʦiʡiʦəɾajto~u ko~u kimaɬ~ɮ no~u kawas!
S.TRANSL: The effect was amazing!

S.FORM: Kina pinapinaay tayra i loma' a miliclic ko tayring, mimaanay kamo sanay.
Kina pinapinaay tayra i loma' a miliclic ko tayring, mimaanay kamo sanay.
S.PHON: kina pinapinaaj tajra i ɾo~umaʡ a miɾiʦɾiʦ ko~u tajriŋ, mimaanaj kamo~u sanaj.
S.TRANSL: A couple of times, the police came to the house and demanded to know what we were doing.

S.FORM: Saka pito romi'ad, kina pito a mitaliyok to niyaro'.
Saka pito romi'ad, kina pito a mitaliyok to niyaro'.
S.PHON: saka pito~u ro~umiʡaɬ~ɮ, kina pito~u a mitaɾijo~uk to~u nijaro~uʡ.

S.FORM: Salikaka Loso kina pitoay a miliso' to Aielan.
Salikaka Loso kina pitoay a miliso' to Aielan.
S.PHON: saɾikaka ɾo~uso~u kina pito~uaj a miɾiso~uʡ to~u aiəɾan.
S.TRANSL: Brother Russell visited Ireland seven times in all.

S.FORM: Kina cecayay, o salikaka i Tokmok mipohayay tamiyanan mirienang to pakaetimay.
Kina cecayay, o salikaka i Tokmok mipohayay tamiyanan mirienang to pakaetimay.
S.PHON: kina ʦəʦajaj, o~u saɾikaka i to~ukmo~uk mipo~uħajaj tamijanan miriənaŋ to~u pakaətimaj.

Misamaan kiso sini'ada to tamdaw i kalo a romi'ad?

S.FORM: Nga'ay kiso misa'icel patatodong to palinian mirienang to SKE.
Nga'ay kiso misa'icel patatodong to palinian mirienang to SKE.
S.PHON: ŋaʡaj kiso~u misaʡiʦəɾ patato~uɬ~ɮo~uŋ to~u paɾinian miriənaŋ to~u skə.

Misarocod mirienang to mitosilay mapakalalok kiso, kaleki mananam i faelohay kitiraan.

S.TRANSL: You will be invigorated and will adapt quicker when you share fully in the ministry.

S.PHON: wa ŋaʡaj kiso~u misamaan a mananam i faəɾo~uħaj a kasako~upaŋ?
S.TRANSL: How Can You Adjust to Your New Congregation?

S.FORM: Sowalen samaanay kiso misaka dademak to kato no JW.
Sowalen samaanay kiso misaka dademak to kato no JW.
S.PHON: so~uwaɾən samaanaj kiso~u misaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmak to~u kato~u no~u *w.

S.FORM: Pafeli to 'orip, paco'ayen Kiso.
Pafeli to 'orip, paco'ayen Kiso.
S.PHON: pafəɾi to~u ʡo~urip, paʦo~uʡajən kiso~u.
S.TRANSL: To thank you, Jehovah, for the life that I live?

S.FORM: Nanay rahekeren Kiso,
Nanay rahekeren Kiso,
S.PHON: nanaj raħəkərən kiso~u,
S.TRANSL: The meditation of my heart,

Yihofaaw, ko'idayto Kiso!"
S.PHON: jiħo~ufaaw, ko~uʡiɬ~ɮajto~u kiso~u!"

S.FORM: Maharateng Kiso.
Maharateng Kiso.

S.TRANSL: a heart that won't forget,

S.FORM: "Nanay damaen ni Yihofa kiso, pa'icelen Ningra kiso."
"Nanay damaen ni Yihofa kiso, pa'icelen Ningra kiso."
S.PHON: "nanaj ɬ~ɮamaən ni jiħo~ufa kiso~u, paʡiʦəɾən niŋra kiso~u."
S.TRANSL: "May Jehovah sustain you at this time of such great loss."

Mihaenay, wa samaan kiso misaka dademak to cowantan a masasowal to tao hani?
S.PHON: miħaənaj, wa samaan kiso~u misaka ɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮəmak to~u ʦo~uwantan a masaso~uwaɾ to~u tao~u ħani?

S.TRANSL: "Blessed Be Your Good Sense!"

Mamafana' kiso to ca'of nora ilaenoay salicay:
S.PHON: mamafanaʡ kiso~u to~u ʦaʡo~uf no~ura iɾaəno~uaj saɾiʦaj:

O roma, pasowalan no coyoh ci Taniil: "I paherekan a romi'ad i, o lalomowad kiso a ma'orip.

S.TRANSL: The message to the prophet Daniel continued: "You will stand up for your lot at the end of the days."

S.FORM: Sinanoten miharateng, o nidemakan ni Yihofa nohatini, oha mamisamaan kiso misa'icel a mipadang.
Sinanoten miharateng, o nidemakan ni Yihofa nohatini, oha mamisamaan kiso misa'icel a mipadang.
S.PHON: sinano~utən miħaratəŋ, o~u niɬ~ɮəmakan ni jiħo~ufa no~uħatini, o~uħa mamisamaan kiso~u misaʡiʦəɾ a mipaɬ~ɮaŋ.

Mafana'ay Kiso Hakowaay ko Toki Nohatini?
